The creature moves with the crawling grave , has a {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} of 0 that cannot be increased, and has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on {@variantrule Death Saving Throw|XPHB}. A creature that loses the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition or is revived stops moving with the crawling grave. A creature within 5 feet of the crawling grave can take an action to pull a creature or corpse free of the crawling grave with a successful {@dc 13} Strength ({@skill Athletics}) check.

At the end of the crawling grave's second turn after picking up a corpse, the corpse breaks apart and the crawling grave regains {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to half that corpse's former {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximum or 20 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, whichever is fewer. The crawling grave expels anything the corpse was wearing or carrying.

trait.name: Swarmlike
entries: The crawling grave can occupy another creature's space and vice versa, and the crawling grave can move through any opening large enough for a Small creature.

trait.name: Undead Fortitude
entries: If damage reduces the crawling grave to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, it must make a Constitution saving throw with a DC of 5 plus the damage taken unless the damage is Radiant or from a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB}. {@actSaveSuccess} The crawling grave drops to 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} instead.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The crawling grave makes two Slam attacks

action.name: Slam
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 5}, reach 5 ft. {@h}14 ({@damage 2d10 + 3}) Bludgeoning damage, and if the target is Medium or smaller, it has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition. If the crawling grave is {@status Bloodied|XPHB}, it deals 8 ({@damage 1d10 + 3}) Bludgeoning damage and doesn't impose the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition.

monster.name: Withered Steed
languages: understands one language its creator speaks but can't speak

trait.name: Immutable Form
entries: The withered steed can't shape-shift.

action.name: Hooves
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 6}, reach 5 ft. {@h}9 ({@damage 2d4 + 4}) Bludgeoning damage plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) Cold damage.

monster.name: Ghost Mustang
languages: understands one language its creator speaks but can't speak

trait.name: Undead Fortitude
entries: If damage reduces the ghost mustang to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, it must make a Constitution saving throw (DC 5 plus the damage taken), unless the damage is Radiant or from a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB}. On a success, the ghost mustang drops to 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} instead.

action.name: Hooves
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 6}, reach 5 ft. {@h}9 ({@damage 2d4 + 4}) Bludgeoning damage plus 10 ({@damage 3d6}) Necrotic damage.

action.name: Hooves
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 6}, reach 5 ft. {@h}9 ({@damage 2d4 + 4}) Bludgeoning damage and the target has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition if it is Medium or smaller.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 6}, reach 5 ft. {@h}9 ({@damage 2d4 + 4}) Piercing damage plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) Fire damage.

trait.name: Siege Monster
entries: The daemon oak deals double damage to objects and structures.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The daemon oak makes two attacks, using Lash or Rock in any combination. It can replace one attack with a use of Spew.

action.name: Lash
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 10}, reach 10 ft. {@h}23 ({@damage 5d6 + 6}) Bludgeoning damage, and if the target is Large or smaller, it has the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition (escape {@dc 18}). While {@condition Grappled|XPHB}, the target has the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition. The daemon oak can grapple four creatures at a time.

action.name: Rock
entries: {@atkr r} {@hit 10}, range 60/180 ft. {@h}32 ({@damage 4d12 + 6}) Bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Spew {@recharge 5}
entries: {@actSave dex} {@dc 17}, each creature in a 30-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone}. {@actSaveFail} 27 ({@damage 6d8}) Acid damage. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage.

action.name: Tree Thrall (Recharges after a Short or Long Rest)
entries: The daemon oak curses and animates one tree within 30 feet. The tree becomes a {@creature Daemon Oak Thrall|GrimHollowMG24}. This thrall is the daemon oak's ally, and it takes its turn just after the daemon oak. The thrall obeys the daemon oak's verbal commands (no action required for the daemon oak). If issued no commands, the thrall attacks a creature that isn't the thrall's ally. A thrall dies after 24 hours, when the daemon oak uses a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to sever the connection to the thrall, the thrall is more than 1 mile from the daemon oak, or the daemon oak dies.

reaction.name: Chain
entries: {@actTrigger} A creature within 10 feet of the daemon oak starts to move. {@actResponse} The daemon oak makes a Lash attack against the triggering creature.

monster.name: Daemon Oak Thrall
languages: understands Infernal and Sylvan but can't speak
monster.cr: — with a daemon oak (3 [700] by itself)

trait.name: Caustic Demise
entries: {@actSave dex} {@dc 16}, each creature in a 10-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from the thrall when it dies. {@actSaveFail} 13 ({@damage 3d8}) Acid damage. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage.

trait.name: Siege Monster
entries: The daemon oak thrall deals double damage to objects and structures.

action.name: Slam
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 9}, reach 5 ft. {@h}16 ({@damage 3d6 + 6}) Bludgeoning damage.

trait.name: Parasitic Release
entries: {@actSave con} {@dc 16}, each creature in a 10-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from the danxoni when it dies. The {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} spreads around corners. {@actSaveFail} The target is infected with {@disease Sewer Plague|XDMG}.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The danxoni makes four Claws attacks and one Bite attack.

action.name: Claws
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 9}, reach 10 ft. {@h}12 ({@damage 2d6 + 5}) Slashing damage.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 9}, reach 10 ft. {@h}14 ({@damage 2d8 + 5}) Piercing damage plus 5 ({@damage 2d4}) Acid damage.

action.name: Symbiont Breath {@recharge 5}
entries: {@actSave con} {@dc 16}, each creature in a 60-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone}. {@actSaveFail} 14 ({@damage 4d6}) Acid damage plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) Piercing damage and 14 ({@damage 4d6}) Poison damage. {@actSaveFailBy 5} The target is infected with {@disease Sewer Plague|XDMG}. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage only.

trait.name: Camouflage
entries: The dark mist stalker can {@action Hide|XPHB} while {@variantrule Lightly Obscured|XPHB} by foliage, heavy rain, mist, or other natural phenomena. If it misses with a ranged attack while it has the {@condition Invisible|XPHB} condition, it does not lose that condition.

trait.name: Dead Eye
entries: The dark mist stalker's attacks with Ranged weapons ignore {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Half Cover} and {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Three Quarters Cover}, and score a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} on a roll of 19 or 20 on the d20.

trait.name: Hunter's Stride
entries: The dark mist stalker ignores {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB}.

trait.name: Night Stalker
entries: The dark mist stalker treats {@variantrule Darkness|XPHB} within the range of its {@sense Darkvision|XPHB} as {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB}.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The dark mist stalker makes three attacks, using Skinning Knife or Hunting Bow in any combination.

action.name: Skinning Knife
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 9}, reach 5 ft. {@h}14 ({@damage 2d8 + 5}) sSlashing damage. {@actSave con} {@dc 16}. {@actSaveFail} The target has the {@condition Bleeding|GrimHollowPG24} condition.

action.name: Hunting Bow
entries: {@atkr r} {@hit 9}, range 150/600 ft. {@h}18 ({@damage 3d8 + 5}) Piercing damage, and the target is pushed 10 feet straight away from the dark mist stalker

action.name: Aim
entries: The dark mist stalker chooses a creature it can see and maintains {@status Concentration|XPHB}. While doing so, creatures have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls against the dark mist stalker. If the dark mist stalker is still maintaining {@status Concentration|XPHB} when it makes its next Hunting Bow attack against the target, the attack has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} and deals an extra 27 ({@damage 6d8}) Piercing damage.

reaction.name: Skirmisher
entries: {@actTrigger} A Hostile creature moves within 5 feet of the dark mist stalker. {@actResponse} The dark mist stalker makes a Skinning Knife attack against the triggering creature, then moves up to 20 feet without provoking {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks}.

trait.name: Sunlight Weakness
entries: While in sunlight, the darkfeather has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on D20 Tests.

action.name: Beak
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 4}, reach 5 ft. {@h}4 ({@damage 1d4 + 2}) Piercing damage. {@actSave con} {@dc 12}. {@actSaveFail} 18 ({@damage 4d8}) Poison damage. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage. {@actSaveSuccessOrFail} If this damage reduces the target to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, the target is stabilized and has the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition for 1 hour. While {@condition Poisoned|XPHB}, the target has the {@condition Paralyzed|XPHB} condition.

bonus.name: Shadow Blend (Recharges after a Short or Long Rest)
entries: While in {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} or {@variantrule Darkness|XPHB}, the darkfeather has the {@condition Invisible|XPHB} condition, along with anything it is wearing or carrying. The condition lasts until the darkfeather attacks, is in {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB}, has the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition, or uses a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to end it.

trait.name: Light Sense
entries: The dawndrinker can sense any light source within 120 feet. This sense penetrates most barriers, but it is blocked by 3 feet of wood or dirt, 1 foot of stone, or 1 inch of metal.

trait.name: Radiant Absorption
entries: When the dawndrinker would take Radiant damage, it instead takes no damage and regains {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the Radiant damage dealt.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The dawndrinker makes two Slam attacks.

action.name: Slam
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 7}, reach 5 ft. {@h}8 ({@damage 1d8 + 4}) Bludgeoning damage plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) Cold damage. {@actSave con} {@dc 13}. {@actSaveFail} The target has the {@condition Blinded|XPHB} condition until the end of the dawndrinker's next turn.

bonus.name: Drink Light
entries: The dawndrinker drinks light, extinguishing nonmagical light sources within 30 feet. Magical lights in the area created by spells of level 3 or lower, or by creatures of {@variantrule Challenge Rating|XPHB|CR} 7 or lower, are dimmed: {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} is reduced to {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB}, and {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} is extinguished. An extinguished magical light is dispelled, or suppressed for 1 minute if the light source is continuous.

The dawndrinker can extinguished stronger magical lights by succeeding on a Constitution check with a DC of 10 plus the spell's level or 5 plus the creature's {@variantrule Challenge Rating|XPHB|CR}, whichever is lower.

bonus.name: Shadow Cunning
entries: In {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} or {@variantrule Darkness|XPHB}, the dawndrinker can take the {@action Disengage|XPHB} or {@action Hide|XPHB} action as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.

trait.name: False Appearance
entries: While a death cap remains motionless and isn't possessing a creature, the cap is indistinguishable from an ordinary object.

action.name: Crush
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 5}, reach 5 ft. {@h}3 ({@damage 1d4 + 1}) Bludgeoning damage, and the death cap attaches to the target. While attached, the death cap can't attack creatures other than the target, but it has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls. The death cap's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} becomes 0, it can't benefit from any bonus to its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}, and it moves with the target.
A creature can take an action to remove the attached death cap with a successful {@dc 15} Strength ({@skill Athletics}) check. The death cap can detach itself using 5 feet of movement.

action.name: Possession
entries: {@actSave cha} {@dc 14}, one creature the death cap is touching. {@actSaveFail} The target is possessed by the death cap. The death cap attaches to the target, as with its Crush attack, and the target loses control of its body. The death cap retains its mental ability scores, {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB|Immunities}, and senses, but otherwise uses the target's statistics, gaining access to the target's knowledge, class features, proficiencies, and languages. The death cap can possess one target at a time and can't use its Crush attack while possessing a target.

The possessed target deals one extra die of damage with melee weapons. At the start of its turn, the death cap can grant the target {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on melee attack rolls until the start of its next turn. Attack rolls against the target have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} for the same duration.

A creature can detach the death cap only if the target has the {@condition Grappled|XPHB}, {@condition Restrained|XPHB}, or {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} conditions. The attempt provokes an {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB} from the target. If the death cap is detached, the possession ends.

Every 24 hours, the death cap drains one of the target's {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB|Hit Dice}, which it regains only after the possession ends. If the target has no {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB|Hit Dice}, it dies. After losing a {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB|Hit Die} in this way, the target can repeat the save, ending the possession on a success. The possession also ends if the target drops to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, the death cap is turned, or the death cap ends the possession as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. When the possession ends, the death cap falls to the ground in an unoccupied space within 5 feet and the target is immune to the death cap's Possession for 24 hours.

variant.name: Other Slayers
entries: While a cap appears to be the favored form of these horrid creatures, stories abound about other forms, from cloaks that seem more patient than the cap form to gauntlets or even weapons, more interested in political power than pure slaughter. These different types of death caps keep their host around for extended periods, sometimes even years, which means the death cap either doesn't feed on the host or keeps them alive with some form of necromancy.
Some necromancers and priests educated in the dark arts either scoff at such tales or warn that the cap form might be a lesser form of this item-haunting undead.

trait.name: Amorphous
entries: The ooze can move through a space as narrow as 1 inch without expending extra movement to do so.

trait.name: Amphibious
entries: The ooze can breathe air and water.

trait.name: False Appearance
entries: While the ooze remains motionless, it is indistinguishable from a mass of waste.

trait.name: Infection
entries: A non-Ooze creature that starts its turn within 10 feet of the ooze or touches it is subjected to the following effect. {@actSave con} {@dc 13}. {@actSaveFail} The target has the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition until the start of its next turn. {@actSaveFailBy 5} The target also contracts {@disease Sewer Plague|XDMG}. {@actSaveSuccess} The target is immune to the Infection of all urban oozes for 1 hour and to {@disease Sewer Plague|XDMG} for 24 hours.

action.name: Pseudopod
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 4}, reach 5 ft. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d6 + 2}) Bludgeoning damage plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) Poison damage, and the target is subjected to Infection.

reaction.name: Diminish
entries: {@actTrigger} The ooze becomes {@status Bloodied|XPHB}. {@actResponse} The ooze splits into two {@creature Diminished Urban Oozes|GrimHollowMG24}. Each new ooze acts on the original ooze's {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB}. The original ooze's {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} are divided evenly between the new oozes (round down).

trait.name: Amorphous
entries: The ooze can move through a space as narrow as 1 inch without expending extra movement to do so.

trait.name: Amphibious
entries: The ooze can breathe air and water.

trait.name: False Appearance
entries: While the ooze remains motionless, it is indistinguishable from a mass of waste.

trait.name: Infection
entries: A non-Ooze creature that starts its turn within 10 feet of the ooze or touches it is subjected to the following effect. {@actSave con} {@dc 12}. {@actSaveFail} The target has the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition until the start of its next turn. {@actSaveFailBy 5} The target also contracts {@disease Sewer Plague|XDMG}. {@actSaveSuccess} The target is immune to the Infection of all urban oozes for 1 hour and to {@disease Sewer Plague|XDMG} for 24 hours.

trait.name: Spider Climb
entries: The ooze can climb difficult surface, including upside down on ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

action.name: Pseudopod
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 3}, reach 5 ft. {@h}3 ({@damage 1d4 + 1}) Bludgeoning damage plus 2 ({@damage 1d4}) Poison damage, and the target is subjected to Infection

trait.name: Amorphous

trait.name: Amphibious
entries: The ooze can breathe air and water.

entries: While the ooze remains motionless, it is indistinguishable from a mass of waste.

trait.name: Infection
