entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 5}, reach 5 ft. {@h}10 ({@damage 2d6 + 3}) Slashing damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature and the bat flew 20+ feet straight toward it immediately before the hit, the target has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition.

monster.name: Styrkvisnar
languages: Any three languages (can't speak in Hybrid Form)

trait.name: Adaptable Warrior
entries: The styrkvisnar has proficiency with all weapons.

trait.name: Hold Breath
entries: The styrkvisnar can hold its breath for 30 minutes.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (1/Day)
entries: If the styrkvisnar fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The styrkvisnar makes three attacks, using Bite, Talons, Tail, or Unarmed Strikes in any combination.

action.name: Bite (Hybrid Form Only)
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 6}, reach 5 ft. {@h}8 ({@damage 2d4 + 3}) Piercing damage.

action.name: Tail (Hybrid Form Only)
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 6}, reach 5 ft. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d8 + 3}) Bludgeoning damage, and if the target is Medium or smaller, it has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition.

action.name: Talons (Hybrid Form Only)
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 6}, reach 5 ft. {@h}8 ({@damage 2d4 + 3}) Slashing damage.

action.name: Unarmed Strike
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 6}, reach 5 ft. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d8 + 3}) Bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Thunderous Roar {@recharge}
entries: {@actSave dex} {@dc 14}, each creature in a 30-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone}. {@actSaveFail} 36 ({@damage 8d8}) Thunder damage. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage. {@actSaveSuccessOrFail} The roar is audible over 1 mile.

action.name: Shape-Shift
entries: The styrkvisnar shape-shifts into a Medium Humanoid or back into its true form, which is a hybrid of eagle, seal, and wolf. Its game statistics are the same in each form. Any equipment it is wearing or carrying isn't transformed.

legendary.name: Attack
entries: The styrkvisnar uses Bite, Tail, Talon, or Unarmed Strike.

legendary.name: Move
entries: The styrkvisnar moves up to its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} without provoking {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks}.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The sunwraith has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The sunwraith makes two Greatsword attacks. It can replace one attack with a use of Life Burn it available.

action.name: Greatsword
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 10}, reach 5 ft. {@h}19 ({@damage 4d6 + 5}) Force damage plus 18 ({@damage 1d4}) Necrotic or Radiant damage (sunwraith's choice).

action.name: Life Burn {@recharge}
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 10}, reach 5 ft. or range 120 ft {@h}30 ({@damage 6d8 + 3}) Necrotic or Radiant damage (sunwraith's choice). The target's {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximum is reduced by an amount equal to the damage taken. A Humanoid killed by this effect rises as a specter at the start of the sunwraith's next turn. The specter is under the sunwraith's direct control. The sunwraith can control seven specters at a time.

reaction.name: Blinding Corona
entries: {@actTrigger} A creature that can see the sunwraith harms it with an attack or effect from more than 5 feet away. {@actResponse}—{@actSave con} {@dc 16}. {@actSaveFail} The target has the {@condition Blinded|XPHB} condition. At the end of each of its turns, the target repeats the save, ending the effect on itself on a success. After 1 minute, it automatically succeeds.

monster.name: Suture Golem
languages: understands the languages of its creator but can't speak

trait.name: Immutable Form
entries: The suture golem can't shape-shift.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The suture golem has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The suture golem makes two Slam attacks. It can replace one attack with a use of Suture Whip.

action.name: Slam
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 6}, reach 5 ft. {@h}13 ({@damage 4d4 + 3}) Force damage.

action.name: Suture Whip
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 6}, reach 15 ft. {@h}8 ({@damage 2d4 + 3}) Force damage. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it has the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition (escape {@dc 14}) and is pulled into an unoccupied space within 5 feet of the suture golem. Until the grapple ends, the suture golem can't make Suture Whip attacks against another target.

If the suture golem hits a Medium or smaller target with Suture Whip while the target has the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition and is within 5 feet of the suture golem, the target is pulled into the suture golem's space and sutured to its body. The grapple ends and the target has the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition. While {@condition Restrained|XPHB}, the target moves with the suture golem, and the suture golem can't make attacks against it. It can escape from the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition in the same fashion as the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition, but has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on its check to do so.

The suture golem can have up to two Medium or smaller creatures sutured to its body at a time. While it has two creatures trapped in this way, its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is halved. If the suture golem dies, the sutures dissolve, freeing the creatures.

bonus.name: Suture Constrict
entries: Each creature {@condition Restrained|XPHB} by the suture golem takes 8 ({@damage 2d4 + 3}) Bludgeoning damage.

trait.name: False Appearance
entries: While the svangras remains motionless, it is indistinguishable from an ordinary wild onion. The Sapping Grass action doesn't disrupt this disguise.

action.name: Tendril
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 2}, reach 5 ft. {@h}2 ({@damage 1d4}) Necrotic damage.

action.name: Sapping Grass (1/Day)
entries: {@actSave con} {@dc 10}, each non-Plant creature in a 20-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from the svangras. {@actSaveFail} 5 ({@damage 2d4}) Necrotic damage.{@actSaveFailBy 5} The target also suffers an {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} level.

trait.name: Keen Hearing
entries: The swarm of ears has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks that rely on hearing.

trait.name: Pain Transfer
entries: When the swarm takes damage and creatures {@condition Charmed|XPHB} by it are in its space, divide the damage in half. The swarm takes one half, and the other half is divided among the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} creatures.

trait.name: Sound Dependent
entries: While the swarm is {@condition Deafened|XPHB}, creatures have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against the swarm's Overwhelming Whispers.

trait.name: Swarm
entries: The swarm of ears can occupy another creature's space and vice versa, and the swarm can move through any opening large enough for a Tiny creature. The swarm can't regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} or gain{@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB}.

trait.name: Waxy Discharge
entries: The swarm's space is covered with viscous wax that dries quickly as the swarm moves. This area is {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB} for creatures other than swarms of ears and creatures {@condition Charmed|XPHB} by them.

action.name: Overwhelming Whispers
entries: {@actSave wis} {@dc 15}, each creature in the swarm's space. {@actSaveFail} 22 ({@damage 4d10}) Psychic damage, or 11 ({@damage 2d10}) Psychic damage if the swarm has the {@status Bloodied|XPHB} condition, and the target has the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} condition until the start of the swarm's next turn. While {@condition Charmed|XPHB}, the target must use its {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to move with the swarm, remaining in its space if possible. Movement used in this way is still expended on the target's next turn.

action.name: Sacrifice
entries: While in the kokela's space, the swarm of ears can destroy itself. The kokela regains {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the swarm's remaining {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, and the swarm dies.

monster.name: The Churning

trait.name: Enormous Space
entries: The Churning takes up an entire 60-foot {@variantrule Cube [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cube}. Other creatures can move through this space, but a creature that does so is subjected to Engulf.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The Churning has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Siege Monster
entries: The Churning deals double damage to objects and structures.

trait.name: Unstoppable
entries: The Churning ignores {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB}, and nothing can reduce its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} or stop it from moving on its turn.

action.name: Pseudopod
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 15}, reach 20 ft. {@h}43 ({@damage 6d10 + 10}) Bludgeoning damage, and the target is pulled into a space within 5 feet of the Churning.

action.name: Engulf
entries: The Churning moves up to its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} and can move through the spaces of other creatures, subjecting each of them to the following effect. Strength or {@actSave dex} {@dc 23}. {@actSaveFail} 40 ({@damage 10d6 + 5}) Bludgeoning damage, and the target is pulled into the Churning's body. The target has the {@condition Blinded|XPHB} and {@condition Restrained|XPHB} conditions, is suffocating, and has {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Total Cover} from effects that originate outside the Churning. At the start of each of the Churning's turns, the target takes 42 ({@damage 12d6}) Bludgeoning damage.

The target can escape the Churning by taking an action and succeeding on a {@dc 23} Strength ({@skill Athletics}) or Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics}) check. Escaping the Churning costs 15 feet of movement and ejects the target into a space of its choice within 5 feet of the Churning. If the chapped brute dies, the target no longer has the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition and can escape without using an action or making a check.
If the target dies while engulfed, its body breaks up and the Churning keeps anything it was wearing or carrying.

reactionHeader: The Churning can take up to four {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB|Reactions} per round but only one per turn.

reaction.name: Grab
entries: {@actTrigger} A creature moves while within reach of the Churning's Pseudopod attack. {@actResponse} The Churning uses Pseudopod.

trait.name: Demiplane
entries: The subject content of the tachna becomes a 40-foot {@variantrule Cube [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cube}-shaped sentient demiplane designed to lure and trap victims. A creature within a 10-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} is subjected to the following effect. {@actSave cha} {@dc 13} (a target touching the tachnaautomatically fails). {@actSaveFail} The target is pulled into the tachna's demiplane, arriving in a random unoccupied space. The target canstill attack the tachna from within it, and can't leave the demiplane by any means until the tachna is defeated. {@actSaveSuccess} The target is immune to this trait for 1 hour.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The tachna uses Tremor, if available, and makes two Tentacle attacks.

action.name: Tentacle
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 6}, reach 10 ft. {@h}12 ({@damage 2d8 + 3}) Slashing damage.

action.name: Tremor {@recharge 5}
entries: {@actSave str} {@dc 15}, each creature in the tachna's demiplane. {@actSaveFail} The target has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition.

trait.name: Demiplane
entries: The subject content of the tachna gallery becomes a 60-foot {@variantrule Cube [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cube}-shaped sentient demiplane designed to lure and trap victims. A creature within a 10-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} is subjected to the following effect. {@actSave cha} {@dc 20} (a target touching the tachna gallery automatically fails). {@actSaveFail} The target is pulled into the tachna gallery's demiplane, arriving in a random unoccupied space. The target can still attack the tachna gallery from within it, and can't leave the demiplane by any means until the tachna gallery is defeated. {@actSaveSuccess} The target is immune to this trait for 1 hour.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If the tachna gallery fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Psychic Aura
entries: creature that starts its turn in the tachna gallery's demiplane takes 21 ({@damage 6d6}) Psychic damage.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The tachna gallery uses Tremor and makes three Tentacle attacks.

action.name: Tentacle
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 18}, reach 60 ft. {@h}22 ({@damage 4d8 + 4}) Slashing damage.

action.name: Tremor
entries: {@actSave str} {@dc 20}, each creature in the tachna's demiplane. {@actSaveFail} The target has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition.

action.name: Mind Blast {@recharge}
entries: {@actSave int} {@dc 20}, each creature in a 60-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from the tachna gallery. {@actSaveFail} 54 ({@damage 12d8}) Psychic damage, and the target has the {@condition Stunned|XPHB} condition. At the end of each of its turns, the target repeats the save, ending the effect on itself on a success. After 1 minute, it automatically succeeds.

trait.name: False Appearance
entries: While the tangle weed remains motionless, it is indistinguishable from an ordinary plant.

action.name: Constrict
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 6}, reach 15 ft. {@h}The target takes 8 ({@damage 1d8 + 4}) Bludgeoning damage, and the target has the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition (escape {@dc 14}). While {@condition Grappled|XPHB}, the target has the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition. At the end of the tangle weed's turn, the target is pulled 5 feet toward it.

reaction.name: Whipping Strand
entries: {@actTrigger} A creature {@condition Grappled|XPHB} by the tangle weed is subject to an effect that moves it and ends the {@condition Grappled|XPHB|Grapple}. {@actResponse} The tangle weed makes a Constrict attack against the creature.

trait.name: Amorphous
entries: The tar creep can move through a space as narrow as 1 inch without expending extra movement to do so.

trait.name: Kindle
entries: When the tar creep takes Fire damage, it ignites. While burning, the tar creep's Slam attacks adhere flaming pitch to foes, dealing an extra 4 ({@damage 1d8}) Fire damage and igniting the target. Until a burning creature takes an action to douse the fire, the creature takes 5 ({@damage 1d10}) Fire damage at the start of each of its turns.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The tar creep makes two Slam attacks. It can replace one attack with Engulf.

action.name: Slam
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 7}, reach 10 ft. {@h}17 ({@damage 3d8 + 4}) Bludgeoning damage, and if the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it has the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition (escape {@dc 15}). While {@condition Grappled|XPHB}, the target has the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition. The tar creep can grapple up to two creatures at a time.

action.name: Engulf
entries: {@actSave str} {@dc 15}, one creature {@condition Grappled|XPHB} by the tar creep. {@actSaveFail} The grapple ends and the target is pulled into the tar creep's body. The target has the {@condition Blinded|XPHB} and {@condition Restrained|XPHB} conditions, is suffocating, and has {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Total Cover} from effects that originate outside the tar creep. At the start of each of the blood ooze's turns, the target takes 13 ({@damage 3d8}) Bludgeoning damage plus 5 ({@damage 1d10}) Fire damage if the tar creep is burning. The tar creep can have one creature engulfed at a time.

The target can escape the tar creep by taking an action and succeeding on a {@dc 15} Strength ({@skill Athletics}) or Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics}) check. Escaping the tar creep costs 5 feet of movement and ejects the target into a space of its choice within 5 feet of the tar creep. If the tar creep dies, the target no longer has the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition and can escape without using an action or making a check.
If the target dies while engulfed, its body dissolves and the tar creep expels anything it was wearing or carrying.

trait.name: Amorphous
entries: The tar juggernaut can move through a space as narrow as 1 inch without expending extra movement to do so.

trait.name: Engulfing Charge
entries: If the tar juggernaut moves 20+ feet straight toward a creature immediately before hitting it with a Slam attack, the target is subjected to the following effect. {@actSave str} {@dc 18}. {@actSaveFail} The target has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition, and if it is a Medium or smaller creature, the tar juggernaut can use Engulf against it as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.

trait.name: Kindle
entries: When the tar juggernaut takes Fire damage, it ignites. While burning, the tar juggernaut's Slam attacks adhere flaming pitch to foes, dealing an extra 11 ({@damage 2d10}) Fire damage and igniting the target. Until a burning creature takes an action to douse the fire, the creature takes 11 ({@damage 2d10}) Fire damage at the start of each of its turns.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The tar juggernaut makes three Slam attacks. It can use Engulf in place of any attack.

action.name: Slam
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 10}, reach 15 ft. {@h}22 ({@damage 3d10 + 6}) Bludgeoning damage, and if the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it has the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition (escape {@dc 18}). While {@condition Grappled|XPHB}, the target has the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition. The tar juggernaut can grapple up to two creatures at a time.

action.name: Engulf
entries: {@actSave str} {@dc 18}, one creature {@condition Grappled|XPHB} by the tar juggernaut. {@actSaveFail} The grapple ends and the target is pulled into the tar juggernaut's body. The target has the {@condition Blinded|XPHB} and {@condition Restrained|XPHB} conditions, is suffocating, and has {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Total Cover} from effects that originate outside the tar juggernaut. At the start of each of the blood ooze's turns, the target takes 22 ({@damage 4d10}) Bludgeoning damage plus 11 ({@damage 2d10}) Fire damage if the tar juggernaut is burning. The tar juggernaut can have one Large creature or two Medium or smaller creatures engulfed at a time.

The target can escape the tar juggernaut by taking an action and succeeding on a {@dc 18} Strength ({@skill Athletics}) or Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics}) check. Escaping the tar juggernaut costs 5 feet of movement and ejects the target into a space of its choice within 5 feet of the tar juggernaut. If the tar juggernaut dies, the target no longer has the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition and can escape without using an action or making a check.
If the target dies while engulfed, its body dissolves and the tar juggernaut expels anything it was wearing or carrying.

variant.name: Tar Juggernaut (Ranged)
entries: Ranged attacks might be important for more powerful monsters to have, especially if the powerful characters they oppose have a variety of ways to attack from a distance. The tar juggernaut's Engulfing Charge may help in such cases, but other options can be helpful. If you find the tar juggernaut needs a ranged attack, consider the following:

entries.name: Hurl Tar
entries: {@atkr r} {@hit 10}, range 50/150 ft. {@h}22 ({@damage 3d10 + 6}) Bludgeoning damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, has the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition (escape {@dc 18}). Until this grapple ends, the target makes ranged weapon or spell attacks with {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB}.

trait.name: Thorn Rot
entries: A Humanoid that takes damage from the thornhost's Thorns attack or Burst is subjected to the following effect. {@actSave con} {@dc 12}. {@actSaveFail} 2 ({@damage 1d4}) Piercing damage. For 3 rounds, the target repeats the save at the start of each of its turns as the thorn continues to burrow. Third {@actSaveFail} The thorn takes root, the target can't regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} lost to Thorn Rot until it is cured, and the target suffers an {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} level.

During the first 24 hours, a creature can take an action to remove the thorn rot with a successful {@dc 15} Wisdom ({@skill Medicine}) check. Otherwise, at the end of each {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, the target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw ({@dc 12} plus the number of thorns, up to {@dc 15}) or suffer an {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} level. If the target dies from {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB}, its soul departs its still-living body and becomes a thornhost.

action.name: Thorns
entries: {@atkr m,r} {@hit 3}, reach 5 ft. or range 20/60 ft. {@h}14 ({@damage 3d8 + 1}) Piercing damage.

action.name: Burst
entries: {@actSave dex} {@dc 12}, each creature in a 20-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from the thornhost. {@actSaveFail} 12 ({@damage 4d4 + 2}) Piercing damage. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage. {@actSaveSuccessOrFail} The thornhust dies.

action.name: Blood Drink
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 5}, reach 5 ft. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d4 + 3}) Piercing damage, and the trennensel attaches to the target. While attached, the trennensel uses its {@variantrule Action|XPHB} to drain blood, and the target loses 2 ({@damage 1d4}) {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}.

The trennensel can detach itself by spending 5 feet of its movement. The target or a creature within 5 feet of it can take an action to detach the trennensel with a successful {@dc 9} Strength ({@skill Athletics}) check. It detaches automatically if it dies or has the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition.

monster.name: Unfinished
languages: understands all languages it knew in life but can't speak

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The unfinished casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 16}, {@hit 8} to spell attacks):

trait.name: Semi-Corporeal
entries: Parts of the unfinished fade in and out of the Material Plane. The unfinished has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saves to avoid or end the {@condition Grappled|XPHB}, {@condition Prone|XPHB}, and {@condition Restrained|XPHB} conditions. It makes another save at the beginning of its turns for an effect that causes any of these conditions, whether it normally allows a saving throw or not.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The unfinished makes two Slam attacks.

action.name: Slam
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 7}, reach 5 ft. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d8 + 3}) Bludgeoning damage plus 17 ({@damage 3d8 + 4}) Necrotic damage. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it is pushed 10 feet and has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition.

monster.name: Vessel Shard
languages: understands one national language and three others but can't speak

trait.name: Spirit Absorption
entries: When a creature dies or is destroyed within 30 feet of the vessel shard, the vessel shard absorbs part of the departing spiritual energy, regaining 10 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} or the dead creature's {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximum, whichever is less.

trait.name: Turn Resistance
entries: The vessel shard has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against any effect that turns undead.

trait.name: Unhallowed Aura
entries: Unless the vessel shard has the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition, it emits a 30-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} of desecration. Undead creatures of its choice in the {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} roll {@dice 1d4} and add that number to attack rolls and saving throws. Halve the number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} a living creature regains while within the {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation}.

A Humanoid that dies in the {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} reanimates as a {@creature Zombie|XMM} at the start of its next turn, provided enough of its body is intact. The vessel shard controls this {@creature Zombie|XMM}.

action.name: Sepulchral Touch
entries: {@atkr m,r} {@hit 6}, reach 5 ft. or ranged 120 ft. {@h}28 ({@damage 8d6)}) Necrotic damage. {@actSave con} {@dc 14}. {@actSaveFail} The target has the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition until the end of the vessel shard's next turn, and each creature of the shard vessel's choice within 5 ft. of the target takes 10 ({@damage 3d6}) Necrotic damage.

action.name: Putrefy {@recharge 5}
entries: {@actSave con} {@dc 14}, each creature in the vessel shard's Unhallowed Aura. {@actSaveFail} 14 ({@damage 4d6}) Necrotic damage, and the target takes another 3 ({@damage 1d6}) Necrotic damage at the start of each of its turns. At the end of each of its turns, the target repeats the save, ending the ongoing damage on a success. It automatically succeeds after 1 minute. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage only.

action.name: Shattered Spirits (1/Day)
entries: {@actSave wis} {@dc 14}, each creature in the vessel shard's Unhallowed Aura. {@actSaveFail} 10 ({@damage 3d6}) Necrotic damage, and the target is cursed. While cursed, the target can't regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} and its {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximum is reduced by the amount of Necrotic damage taken until the curse ends. It also suffers one of the following delusions (roll {@dice 1d6}), or a similar delusion of the GM's choice:

rows: Only I matter. Others are disposable.
All other creatures should serve me.
Living beings are weak and not to be trusted.
No risk is too great for my personal glory.
I must never put myself in danger of dying.
To survive, I must eat only little creatures that are still alive.

entries: Every 24 hours, the target repeats the save, ending the curse on a success. {@actSaveFail} The target's {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximum is further reduced by 10 ({@damage 3d6}). If this effect kills the target, it rises as a zombie under the vessel shard's control. The zombie approaches the vessel shard until it is in the vessel shard's Unhallowed Aura, attacking any creature that prevents it from doing so.

The curse can be removed by effects that turn undead or end possession. If the effect calls for a saving throw, the curse has a +4 bonus on its save. The curse can also be removed by keeping the target on hallowed ground and in {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} for 24 hours, but the curse compels the target to escape these conditions if possible.

bonus.name: Consume Spirit
entries: {@actSave wis} {@dc 14}, one Undead creature the vessel shard can see within 30 feet (this is considered an effect that turns undead). {@actSaveFail} The target loses 10 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} and is destroyed if this effect reduces it to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}. The vessel shard regains {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the amount the target lost.

reaction.name: Arcane Shield ({@recharge})
entries: {@actTrigger} The vessel shard is hit by an attack or fails a Dexterity saving throw. {@actResponse} The vessel shard adds 5 to its {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB|AC} against that attack or to the result of its saving throw.

skill.special: Proficient in up to three skills from its past life

trait.name: Unnatural Life
entries: The vitebriate suffers none of the frailty of old age.

trait.name: Vitebriance
entries: While on the same plane of existence as a creature {@condition Charmed|XPHB} by it, the vitebriate can reduce the target's {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximum by 1 each day. This reduction lasts until the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} condition ends and the target rests for 7 days without this reduction.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The vitebriate makes two Dagger attacks.

action.name: Dagger
entries: {@atkr m,r} {@hit 4}, reach 5 ft. or range 20/60 ft. {@h}4 ({@damage 1d4 + 2}) Piercing damage plus 4 ({@damage 1d8}) Psychic damage.

action.name: Dominate Person
entries: The vitebriate casts {@spell Dominate Person|XPHB} (save {@dc 13}), requiring no components and using Charisma as its spellcasting ability. Once the vitebriate chooses a target, it can't target other creatures with {@spell Dominate Person|XPHB} for 24 hours. The spell doesn't require {@status Concentration|XPHB}, but ends early if the vitebriate is killed or knocked {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} against its will (trancing or sleeping doesn't end the spell). If the target has been {@condition Charmed|XPHB} for at least 1 hour, it no longer repeats the save when it takes damage.

reaction.name: Life Shield (1/Day)
entries: {@actTrigger} The vitebriate takes damage. {@actResponse} The triggering damage is instead dealt to a creature {@condition Charmed|XPHB} by the vitebriate. Any damage in excess of the creature's remaining {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} is dealt to the vitebriate.

trait.name: Ethereal Sight
entries: The vooran can see 60 feet into the Ethereal Plane when on other planes, and vice versa.

trait.name: Etherealness
entries: The vooran lives entirely on the Ethereal Plane and can only enter other planes by possessing a Beast. It is not visible outside of the Ethereal Plane.

trait.name: Incorporeal Movement
entries: The vooran can move through other creatures and objects as if they were {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB}. It takes 5 ({@damage 1d10}) Force damage if it ends its turn inside an object.

trait.name: Familiar
entries: vooran can connect with another creature to serve as a familiar, forming a telepathic bond with its willing master. While the two are bonded, the master can sense what the vooran senses if they are within 1 mile of each other. When bonded, the master can see the vooran as a ghostly form.

action.name: Animal Possession (1/Day)
entries: {@actSave cha} {@dc 12}, one Beast of {@filter beast of CR 1/8 or lower|bestiary|Challenge Rating=[&0;&1/8]|type=beast} that the vooran can see within 5 feet. {@actSaveFail} The target is possessed by the vooran. The vooran disappears, and the target has the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition and loses control of its body. The vooran now controls the body, but the target retains awareness. The vooran can't be targeted by any attack, spell, or other effect, except ones that specifically target Fey. The vooran's game statistics are the same, except it uses the target's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}, as well as the target's Strength, Dexterity, and Constitution modifiers.

The possession lasts for 1 hour, ending early if the body drops to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} or the vooran ends it as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. When the possession ends, the vooran is forced back into the Ethereal Plane, and the target is immune to this vooran's Animal Possession for 24 hours. If an animal is killed while the vooran possesses it, the vooran must succeed on a {@dc 10} Charisma saving throw or it is permanently destroyed.
{@actSaveSuccess} The target is immune to this vooran's Possession for 24 hours.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The wandering head casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 13}, {@hit 5} to spell attacks):

spellcasting.name: Counterspell (2/Day)
headerEntries: The wandering head casts {@spell Counterspell|XPHB} in response to that spell's trigger, using the spellcasting ability in Spellcasting.

trait.name: Off Balance
entries: The wandering head has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on ability checks and saving throws made to avoid or end the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The wandering head makes one Bite attack and one Slam attack.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 6}, reach 5 ft. {@h}11 ({@damage 2d6 + 4}) Piercing damage.

action.name: Slam
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 6}, reach 5 ft. {@h}9 ({@damage 2d4 + 4}) Bludgeoning damage.

trait.name: Artificial Form
entries: The wechselkind has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition and is immune to disease. It doesn't need to eat, drink, or breathe.

trait.name: Childish Agility
entries: The wechselkind can move through the space of any creature larger than it.

trait.name: Sneak Attack (1/Turn)
entries: The wechselkind deals an extra 3 ({@damage 1d6}) damage when it hits a target with a weapon attack and has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the attack roll, or when the target is within 5 feet of an ally of the wechselkind that doesn't have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition and the wechselkind doesn't have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the attack roll.

action.name: Dagger
entries: {@atkr m,r} {@hit 5}, reach 5 ft. or range 20/60 ft. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d4 + 3}) Piercing damage.

action.name: Faerie Glamour
entries: The wechselkind casts {@spell Disguise Self|XPHB} (save {@dc 11}), requiring no components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability. When it does so, it can disguise itself only as the child it was created to replace.

bonus.name: Cunning Action
entries: The wechselkind takes the {@action Dash|XPHB}, {@action Disengage|XPHB}, or {@action Hide|XPHB} action.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The wechselkind casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 12}, {@hit 4} to spell attacks):

trait.name: Artificial Form
entries: The wechselkind has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition and is immune to disease. It doesn't need to eat, drink, or breathe.

trait.name: Childish Agility
entries: The wechselkind can move through the space of any creature larger than it.

trait.name: Sneak Attack (1/Turn)
entries: The wechselkind deals an extra 17 ({@damage 5d6}) damage when it hits a target with a weapon attack and has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the attack roll, or when the target is within 5 feet of an ally of the wechselkind that doesn't have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition and the wechselkind doesn't have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the attack roll.

action.name: Rapier
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 5}, reach 5 ft. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d8 + 3}) Piercing damage.

action.name: Faerie Glamour
entries: The wechselkind casts {@spell Disguise Self|XPHB} (save {@dc 12}), requiring no components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability. When it does so, it can disguise itself only as the humanoid child it was created to replace.

bonus.name: Cunning Action
entries: The wechselkind takes the {@action Dash|XPHB}, {@action Disengage|XPHB}, or {@action Hide|XPHB} action.

spellcasting.name: Innate Spellcasting
headerEntries: The wechselkind casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 14}, {@hit 6} to spell attacks):

spellcasting.name: Misty Step (2/Day)
headerEntries: The wechselkind casts {@spell Misty Step|XPHB} using the same spellcasting ability as Spellcasting.

spellcasting.name: Misty Step (2/Day)
headerEntries: The wechselkind casts {@spell Counterspell|XPHB} in response to that spell's trigger, using the same spellcasting ability as Spellcasting.

trait.name: Artificial Form
entries: The wechselkind has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition and is immune to disease. It doesn't need to eat, drink, or breathe.

trait.name: Childish Agility
entries: The wechselkind can move through the space of any creature larger than it.

action.name: Multiattack.
entries: The wechselkind makes three attacks, using Dagger or Force Ray in any combination.

action.name: Dagger
entries: {@atkr m,r} {@hit 5}, reach 5 ft. or range 20/60 ft. {@h}4 ({@damage 1d4 + 2}) Piercing damage plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) Force damage.

action.name: Force Ray
entries: {@atkr r} {@hit 6}, range 120 ft. {@h}11 ({@damage 2d10}) Force damage.

action.name: Faerie Glamour
entries: The wechselkind casts {@spell Disguise Self|XPHB}(save {@dc 11}), requiring no components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability. When it does so, it can disguise itself only as the humanoid child it was created to replace.

trait.name: Undead Fortitude
entries: If damage reduces the weeping zombie to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, it makes a Constitution saving throw (DC 5 plus the damage taken), unless the damage is Radiant or from a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB}. On a success, the weeping zombie drops to 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} instead.

trait.name: Weeping Pox
entries: {@actSave con} {@dc 15}, a Humanoid that comes within 5 feet of the weeping zombie. {@actSaveSuccess} The target is immune to {@disease Weeping Pox|GrimHollowCG24} for 24 hours. {@actSaveFail} The target contracts {@disease Weeping Pox|GrimHollowCG24}. The target repeats the save every 24 hours, suffering an {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} level on a failure. This {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} can't be removed until the disease is cured with magic.

Until the first {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} level sets in, the target has sharpened senses. Whenever the target makes an ability check or saving throw against anything other than the disease, it rolls a d4 and adds the result to its roll.

action.name: Slam
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 3}, reach 5 ft. {@h}6 ({@damage 2d4 + 1}) Bludgeoning damage, and the target is subjected to Weeping Pox.

monster.name: Weeping Lord
languages: understands the languages it spoke in life but can't speak

spellcasting.name: Innate Spellcasting
headerEntries: The weeping lord casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Constitution as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 13}, {@hit 5} to spell attacks):

spellcasting.name: Shield (3/Day)
headerEntries: The weeping lord casts {@spell Shield|XPHB} in response to that spell's trigger, using the spellcasting ability from Innate Spellcasting.

trait.name: Command Weeping Zombies
entries: The weeping lord directs weeping zombies within 120 feet of it using a form of limited telepathy.

trait.name: Undead Fortitude
entries: If damage reduces the weeping zombie to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, it makes a Constitution saving throw (DC 5 plus the damage taken), unless the damage is Radiant or from a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB}. On a success, the weeping zombie drops to 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} instead.

trait.name: Weeping Pox
entries: {@actSave con} {@dc 15}, a Humanoid that comes within 5 feet of the weeping zombie. {@actSaveSuccess} The target is immune to {@disease Weeping Pox|GrimHollowCG24} for 24 hours. {@actSaveFail} The target contracts {@disease Weeping Pox|GrimHollowCG24}. The target repeats the save every 24 hours, suffering an {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} level on a failure. This {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} can't be removed until the disease is cured with magic.

Until the first {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} level sets in, the target has sharpened senses. Whenever the target makes an ability check or saving throw against anything other than the disease, it rolls a {@dice d4} and adds the result to its roll.

action.name: Slam
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 4}, reach 5 ft. {@h}9 ({@damage 2d6 + 2}) Bludgeoning damage, and the target is subjected to Weeping Pox.

action.name: Grave Miasma
entries: {@atkr r} {@hit 5}, reach 50 ft. {@h}9 ({@damage 2d8}) Necrotic damage. The target is subjected to Weeping Pox and can't regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} until the start of the weeping lord's next turn.

trait.name: False Appearance
entries: While the whelmer remains motionless, it is indistinguishable from a pool of water.

trait.name: Freeze
entries: If the whelmer takes Cold damage, it partially freezes, reducing its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} by 20 feet until the end of its next turn.

trait.name: Undertow
entries: The whelmer's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is normal when it moves a {@condition Grappled|XPHB} target.

trait.name: Water Form
entries: The whelmer can enter a {@variantrule Hostile [Attitude]|XPHB|Hostile} creature's space and stop there. It can move through a space as narrow as 1 inch without expending extra movement to do so.

action.name: Slam
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 5}, reach 5 ft. {@h}10 ({@damage 2d6 + 3}) Bludgeoning damage, and if the target is a Large or smaller creature, it has the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition (escape {@dc 13}). Until this grapple ends, the target can't breathe and the whelmer can't Slam another target.

action.name: Illusory Distress (Recharges after a Short or Long Rest)
entries: The whelmer creates an illusion of a drowning Humanoid in a space it can see within 120 feet of it. The illusion seems completely real, including sounds, smells, and temperature. The whelmer can use its action to move the illusion to another spot in range and change its sensory aspects. The elemental can change minor aspects, such as sounds, without using an {@variantrule Action|XPHB}.

Physical interaction with the image reveals it to be an illusion, as creatures and objects can pass through it. A creature that uses its {@variantrule Action|XPHB} to examine the image can determine that it's an illusion with a successful {@dc 12} Intelligence ({@skill Investigation}) check. If a creature discerns the illusion for what it is, the creature can see through the image.

monster.name: Witch Owl
languages: understands one national language but can't speak

trait.name: Flyby
entries: The witch owl doesn't provoke {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks} when it flies out of an enemy's reach.

trait.name: Mimicry
entries: The witch owl can mimic owl sounds and Humanoid voices. A creature that hears the sounds can tell they're imitations with a successful {@dc 10} Wisdom ({@skill Insight}) check.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The witch owl makes two attacks: one with its Bite and one with its Talons.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 3}, reach 5 ft. {@h}4 ({@damage 1d6 + 1}) Slashing damage.

action.name: Talons
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 3}, reach 5 ft. {@h}6 ({@damage 2d4 + 1}) Slashing damage, and if the target is a Humanoid, it is subjected to the following effect. {@actSave cha} {@dc 11}. {@actSaveFail} The target is cursed to become a witch owl.

monster.name: Witch Owl Cultist
languages: One national language and one other language

trait.name: Sneak Attack (1/Turn)
entries: The witch owl cultist deals an extra 3 ({@damage 1d6}) damage when it hits a target with a weapon attack and has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the attack roll, or when the target is within 5 feet of an ally of the cultist that doesn't have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition, and the cultist doesn't have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the attack roll.

action.name: Dagger
entries: {@atkr m,r} {@hit 4}, reach 5 ft. or range 20/60 ft. {@h}4 ({@damage 1d4 + 2}) Piercing damage.

bonus.name: Nimble Escape
entries: The witch owl cultist takes the {@action Disengage|XPHB} or {@action Hide|XPHB} action.

monster.name: Witch Owl Hexta
languages: understands one national language but can't speak

trait.name: Flyby
entries: The witch owl hexta doesn't provoke {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks} when it flies out of an enemy's reach.

trait.name: Keen Hearing and Sight
entries: The witch owl has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks that rely on hearing or sight.

trait.name: Mimicry
entries: The witch owl can mimic owl sounds and Humanoid voices. A creature that hears the sounds can tell they're imitations with a successful {@dc 10} Wisdom ({@skill Insight}) check.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The witch owl makes two attacks: one with its Bite and one with its Talons.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 4}, reach 5 ft. {@h}7 ({@damage 2d4 + 2}) Slashing damage.

action.name: Talons
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 4}, reach 5 ft. {@h}9 ({@damage 2d6 + 2}) Slashing damage, and if the target is a Humanoid, it is subjected to the following effect. {@actSave cha} {@dc 11}. {@actSaveFail} The target is cursed to become a witch owl.

action.name: Illusory Appearance
entries: The witch owl casts {@spell Disguise Self|XPHB} (save {@dc 12}), requiring no components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability.

trait.name: Keen Hearing and Smell
entries: The wolfentroika has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks that rely on hearing or smell.

trait.name: Shared Life
entries: Each of the wolfentroikas in a trio shares life. If the wolfentroika starts its turn with 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} and within 100 feet of another member of its trio that has 1+ {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, the wolfentroika regains 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point}. If a wolfentroika is at 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} and at least one member of its trio is alive, the wolfentroika can die only if its body is destroyed in such a way that only the {@spell True Resurrection|XPHB} spell or similarly powerful magic could restore it to life.

trait.name: Shared Mind
entries: Each of the wolfentroikas in a trio shares consciousness. While within 100 feet of one another, if one wolfentroika is aware of something, all three are. The wolfentroika can have the {@condition Blinded|XPHB}, {@condition Charmed|XPHB}, {@condition Deafened|XPHB}, {@condition Frightened|XPHB}, {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB}, {@condition Paralyzed|XPHB}, {@condition Petrified|XPHB}, {@condition Stunned|XPHB}, or {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} conditions only if the other two succumb to the same condition or are more than 100 feet from the wolfentroika. When one wolfentroika recovers from one of these conditions or moves within 100 feet of an affected sibling, each other trio member within 100 feet recovers.

trait.name: Trio Tactics
entries: The wolfentroika has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls against a creature if at least one of the wolfentroika's trio is within 5 feet of the creature.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 6}, reach 5 ft. {@h}13 ({@damage 2d8 + 4}) Piercing damage plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) Acid damage, and if the target is a Large or smaller creature, it has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition.

action.name: Spit
entries: {@atkr r} {@hit 4}, range 30 ft. {@h}16 ({@damage 4d6 + 2}) Acid damage.

reaction.name: Stay Down
entries: {@actTrigger} A creature within 5 feet of the wolfentroika stands up, ending the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition. {@actResponse} The wolfentroika makes an {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB} against that creature.

trait.name: False Appearance
entries: While motionless, the wood weird is indistinguishable from a clump of beautiful wildflowers.

action.name: Restful Spores
entries: {@actSave con} {@dc 10}, one creature the wood weird can sense within 10 feet (wood weirds are immune). {@actSaveFail} The target has the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition. While poisoned in the way, the target has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition and its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is 0 and cannot be increased. At the end of each of its turns, the target repeats the save, ending the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition on a success.

bonus.name: Cocoon
entries: A Medium or smaller Humanoid that is {@condition Prone|XPHB} within 10 feet of the wood weird must succeed on a {@dc 11} Strength or Dexterity saving throw. {@actSaveFail} The target has the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition (escape {@dc 11}). While {@condition Grappled|XPHB}, the target has the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition. The wood weird can cocoon only one creature at a time.

At the start of each of the wood weird's turns, the target is subjected to the following effect. {@actSave con} {@dc 11}. {@actSaveFail} 3 ({@damage 1d6}) Piercing damage plus 3 ({@damage 1d6}) Poison damage. If the target dies while cocooned, it lignifies over 24 hours and then rises as a {@creature Wood Weird Seeder|GrimHollowMG24}.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The wood weird uses its Alluring Spores, then makes a Slam attack.

action.name: Slam
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 4}, reach 5 ft. one target. {@h}7 ({@damage 2d4 + 2}) Bludgeoning damage, and if the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition.

action.name: Alluring Spores
entries: {@actSave wis} {@dc 11}, one Humanoid the wood weird can sense within 10 feet. {@actSaveFail} The target has the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} condition. While {@condition Charmed|XPHB}, the target can't take {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB|Reactions} and uses its turns to approach the wood weird, stopping when it is within 5 feet. The target avoids obvious danger, including {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks}. If it can't avoid such danger, the target repeats the save, ending the effect on itself on a success. It also repeats the save if it takes damage or at the end of each of the target's turns. It automatically succeeds after 1 minute or if the wood weird is destroyed.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 2}, reach 5 ft. {@h}1 Piercing damage. {@actSave con} {@dc 9}. {@actSaveFail} 2 ({@damage 1d4}) Psychic damage, and the target has the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition until the end of its next turn.

trait.name: Aetheric Resilience
entries: The swarm has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma saving throws.

trait.name: Aura of Psychic Disruption
entries: The swarm deals 5 Psychic damage to any creature that ends its turn in the same space or within 5 feet of the swarm.

trait.name: Swarm
entries: The swarm can occupy another creature's space and vice versa, and the swarm can move through any opening large enough for a Tiny worm. The swarm can't regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} or gain Temporary {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}.

action.name: Bites
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 3}, reach 0 ft. {@h}10 ({@damage 2d8 + 1}) Piercing damage plus 9 ({@damage 2d8}) Psychic damage, or 10 ({@damage 2d8 + 1}) Piercing damage if the swarm has the {@status Bloodied|XPHB} condition. {@actSave con} {@dc 14}. {@actSaveFail} The target has the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition until the end of its next turn.

action.name: Frightful Presence
entries: {@actSave wis} {@dc 14}, each creature of the swarm's choice in a 60-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from the swarm. {@actSaveFail} The target has the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition. At the end of each of its turns, the target repeats the save, ending the effect on itself on a success. It automatically succeeds after 1 minute. If the creature succeeds on its saving throw or the effect ends for it, it is immune to the swarm's Frightful Presence for 24 hours.

monster.name: Wretched Reveler
languages: the languages it knew in life

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The wretched reveler makes two attacks, using Dart or Tankard in any combination.

action.name: Tankard
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 4}, reach 5 ft. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d6 + 2}) Bludgeoning damage plus 3 ({@damage 1d6}) Poison damage.

action.name: Dart
entries: {@atkr r} {@hit 4}, range 20/60 ft. {@h}4 ({@damage 1d4 + 2}) Bludgeoning damage plus 3 ({@damage 1d6}) Poison damage.

bonus.name: Body Moving
entries: {@actSave cha} {@dc 12}, one creature within 30 feet of the wretched reveler that can hear it singing. {@actSaveFail} The target has the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} condition until the start of the wretched reveler's next turn. While {@condition Charmed|XPHB}, the target uses its movement to dance in place without leaving its space, imposing {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on its attack rolls and Dexterity saving throws. Other creatures have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls against the target while it dances.

The wretched reveler can stop singing at any time, ending the effect. The effect ends early if the wretched reveler is killed or has the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition, or if the target can't hear the wretched reveler.

monster.name: Wretched Driver
languages: the languages it knew in life

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The wretched driver makes two Rapier attacks. It can replace one attack with a Cursed Shout.

action.name: Rapier
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 5}, reach 5 ft. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d8 + 3}) Piercing damage plus 5 ({@damage 2d4}) Necrotic damage.

action.name: Cursed Shout
entries: {@actSave cha} {@dc 13}, two creatures within 30 feet of the wretched driver that can hear it. {@actSaveFail} 10 ({@damage 3d6}) Psychic damage, and the target has the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition until the start of the wretched driver's next turn.

bonus.name: Angry Driving
entries: The wretched driver controls a vehicle. {@actSave dex} {@dc 13}, each other creature on the same vehicle as the wretched driver. {@actSaveFail} The target has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition.

monster.name: Wretched Master of Ceremonies
languages: the languages it knew in life

spellcasting.name: Disguise Self
headerEntries: The wretched master of ceremonies casts {@spell Disguise Self|XPHB} (save {@dc 15}), requiring no components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability.

spellcasting.name: Misty Step
headerEntries: The wretched master of ceremonies casts {@spell Misty Step|XPHB}, requiring no components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability.

trait.name: Turning Defiance
entries: The wretched master of ceremonies and any Undead within 30 feet of it have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against effects that turn undead.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The wretched master of ceremonies makes two Whip attacks. It can replace one attack with Macabre Direction.

action.name: Whip
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 6}, reach 10 ft. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d4 + 3}) Slashing damage plus 10 ({@damage 3d6}) Psychic damage, and if the target is a Medium or smaller, the master moves the target to another unoccupied space within the whip's reach.

action.name: Macabre Direction
entries: {@actSave cha} {@dc 15}, one creature of the wretched master of ceremonies' choice within 30 feet that can hear it. {@actSaveFail} The target has the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} condition until the start of the master's next turn. The master can use the target's {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}, forcing it to make a weapon attack or cast a cantrip at a target of the master's choice.

action.name: Jubilant Thralls (Recharges after a Short or Long Rest)
entries: {@actSave cha} {@dc 15}, each Humanoid of the wretched master of ceremonies' choice within 30 feet of it that can hear it (creatures fighting the master have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB}). {@actSaveFail} The target has the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} condition. While {@condition Charmed|XPHB}, the target has the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition, its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is reduced to 0, and it has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks to perceive creatures other than the master.

Each time the target takes damage, it repeats the save, ending the effect on itself on a success. Otherwise, the effect ends after 1 hour or if the master is destroyed or on a different plane of existence than the target. The master can end the effect early as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.

If the target remains {@condition Charmed|XPHB} for 1 minute, it no longer has the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition and regains its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}. However, it remains {@condition Charmed|XPHB}, regarding the master as someone to be heeded and protected. It takes the master's requests and actions in the most favorable way possible. The target protects the master from those trying to harm it, but takes great risks only if bravery or foolhardiness is part of its normal nature.

trait.name: Magic Absorption
entries: If a xakalonus succeeds on a saving throw against a damaging magical effect and takes no damage, it regains 11 ({@dice 2d10}) {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}. Alternatively, if the xakalonus has all of its {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, the next successful melee attack by the xakalonus deals an additional 11 ({@damage 2d10}) Force damage.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The xakalonus has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The xakalonus makes one Bite attack and one Claws attack.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 7}, reach 5 ft. {@h}13 ({@damage 2d8 + 4}) Piercing damage.

action.name: Claws
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 7}, reach 5 ft. {@h}15 ({@damage 2d10 + 4}) Slashing damage.

action.name: Sonic Bark {@recharge 5}
entries: {@actSave con} {@dc 14}, each creature in a 30-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from the xakalonus that can hear it (other xakalonus and the {@creature Hraptnon|GrimHollowMG24} are immune). {@actSaveFail} 14 ({@damage 4d6}) Thunder damage and the target has the {@condition Stunned|XPHB} condition until the end of its next turn.

trait.name: Assimilation
entries: A creature that drops to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} within 15 feet of the drone is subjected to the following effect. Assimilation can penetrate barriers, but 2 feet of rock, 2 inches of metal, a thin sheet of lead, or a barrier of force blocks it. {@actSave cha} {@dc 13}. {@actSaveFail} The target becomes an {@b Yggdrasili Drone}. If the target is near multiple yggdrasili, it makes a single saving throw against the highest DC among them.

trait.name: Psychic Feedback
entries: {@actSave wis} {@dc 13}, each creature in a 5-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from the drone when it takes Psychic damage. {@actSaveFail} The target takes the same amount of Psychic damage. If the target is near multiple yggdrasili, it makes a single saving throw against the highest DC among them and takes only the highest amount of Psychic damage.

trait.name: Psychic Static
entries: Attempts to read the drone's thoughts fail. {@actSave int} {@dc 13}, a creature that tries to read the drone's thoughts. {@actSaveFail} 8 ({@damage 2d4 + 3}) Psychic damage.

action.name: Slam
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 3}, reach 5 ft. {@h}3 ({@damage 1d4 + 1}) Bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Psychic Lash
entries: {@actSave int} {@dc 13}, one creature the drone can sense within 30 feet. {@actSaveFail} 8 ({@damage 2d4 + 3}) Psychic damage.

trait.name: Assimilation
entries: A creature that drops to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} within 30 feet of the drone is subjected to the following effect. Assimilation can penetrate barriers, but 2 feet of rock, 2 inches of metal, a thin sheet of lead, or a barrier of force blocks it. {@actSave cha} {@dc 15}. {@actSaveFail} The target becomes an {@creature Yggdrasili Drone|GrimHollowMG24}. If the target is near multiple yggdrasili, it makes a single saving throw against the highest DC among them.

trait.name: Cloud
entries: The cloud can occupy another creature's space and vice versa, and the cloud can move through any opening large enough for air to enter, but it can't enter or move through liquid.

trait.name: Psychic Static
entries: Attempts to read the cloud's thoughts fail. {@actSave int} {@dc 13}, a creature that tries to read the cloud's thoughts. {@actSaveFail} 11 ({@damage 2d6 + 4}) Psychic damage.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The cloud uses Psychic Thrash twice.

action.name: Psychic Thrash
entries: {@actSave int} {@dc 15}, one creature the cloud can sense within 60 feet, plus one creature in the cloud's space. {@actSaveFail} 11 ({@damage 2d6 + 4}) Psychic damage. {@actSaveFailBy 5} The target has the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition and repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. It automatically succeeds after 1 minute. The effect ends early if the creature takes damage or a creature takes an action to shake it from its stupor.

trait.name: Aetheric Aura
entries: When a creature within 10 feet of the crown takes Psychic damage, it takes an extra 5 (2d4) Psychic damage.

trait.name: Assimilation
entries: A creature that drops to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} within 60 feet of the cloud is subjected to the following effect. Assimilation can penetrate barriers, but 2 feet of rock, 2 inches of metal, a thin sheet of lead, or a barrier of force blocks it. {@actSave cha} {@dc 15}. {@actSaveFail} The target becomes an {@creature Yggdrasili Drone|GrimHollowMG24}. If the target is near multiple yggdrasili, it makes a single saving throw against the highest DC among them.

trait.name: Psychic Static
entries: Attempts to read the crown's thoughts fail. {@actSave int} {@dc 15}, a creature that tries to read the crown's thoughts. {@actSaveFail} 14 ({@damage 2d8 + 5}) Psychic damage.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The crown makes two Slam attacks. It can replace one attack with a use of Psychic Whip.

action.name: Slam
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 5}, reach 5 ft. {@h}7 ({@damage 2d4 + 2}) Bludgeoning damage plus 2 ({@damage 1d4}) Psychic damage.

action.name: Psychic Whip
entries: {@actSave int} {@dc 15}, one creature the crown can sense within 120 feet. {@actSaveFail} 14 ({@damage 2d8 + 5}) Psychic damage, and the target can't take {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB|Reactions} until the start of its next turn.

action.name: Mind Bomb (Recharges after a Short or Long Rest)
entries: {@actSave int} {@dc 15}, each creature in a 20-foot radius Sphere centered on a point the crown can sense within 120 feet. {@actSaveFail} 22 ({@damage 4d10}) Psychic damage, the target can't take {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB|Reactions}, and each time the target makes an ability check or saving throw, it must roll a d4 and subtract the result from its roll. At the end of each of its turns, the target repeats the save, ending the effect on itself on a success. It automatically succeeds after 1 minute. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage only.

trait.name: Inscrutable
entries: The truthseeker has {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to any effect that would sense its emotions or read its thoughts, as well as any Divination spell that it refuses. Wisdom ({@skill Insight}) checks made to determine the truthseeker's intentions or sincerity have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB}.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The Zabrielite truthseeker has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The truthseeker makes two attacks, using Talons or Eye Beams in any combination.

action.name: Talons
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 9}, reach 5 ft. Hit 22 ({@damage 3d10 + 6}) Slashing damage.

action.name: Eye Beams
entries: {@atkr rs} {@hit 9}, range 120 ft. {@h}27 ({@damage 5d10}) Radiant damage. This attack ignores all but {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Total Cover}, and the truthseeker doesn't have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the attack roll while adjacent to an enemy.

action.name: Transfixing Gaze
entries: {@actSave wis} DC 17, one creature the truthseeker can see within 60 feet. {@actSaveFail} The target has the {@condition Paralyzed|XPHB} condition. While {@condition Paralyzed|XPHB}, the truthseeker can read the target's mind to learn its surface thoughts and gain insight into its motivations, emotional state, and major concerns. The target repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

The truthseeker must maintain {@status Concentration|XPHB} on this effect or it immediately ends. While maintaining {@status Concentration|XPHB} on Transfixing Gaze, the truthseeker can't make Eye Beams attacks.
Everything in this damned land tries to kill you: that's my experience. But every once in a while you find a true friend. They only try to kill you if you deserve it.

entries.name: Exceptional Individuals
entries: Etharis's citizens are simply trying to survive in a hostile, unforgiving world. Some are good, a few are evil, but most have neither the power nor the imagination to stand out. That's what heroes are for.

There are some, however, who have intriguing stories and the ability to strike out on their own. These individuals have something important to do, but they may not be able to manage these grand tasks on their own. These nonplayer characters make perfect companions.

entries.name: Useful Friends
entries: Companions like the ones presented here are useful in a variety of ways. If a group of characters is lacking in numbers or in particular skill sets, these companions can supplement a party. They also have their own stories, which can carry some of the narrative weight of an adventure or campaign. They can be used in the short term to introduce new adventures, or in the long term to act as an important piece for the game master's part of the collective story.

She's an odd one, that barmaid. Downright obsessed with every murder what happens in this forsaken city and always so modestly dressed because she's covered in stitches underneath. Least, that's what they say.

entries.name: Spark of Life
entries: Created by an eccentric inventor in the Raevan countryside, Andora spent her early years isolated from the outside world in his sprawling manor. The inventor had long ago been expelled from polite society in Raevo for his experiments on the dead, and he frequently opined about the small-mindedness of his contemporaries. He believed that when he finished Andora's education he would reveal her to his peers in Castalore and be applauded for his genius. He may have been, had he not died of natural causes before he considered Andora's education complete.

entries.name: Failed Attempt
entries: As part of her education, Andora learned etiquette, estate management, and several foreign languages—but not even the basics of her inventor's craft. After years of careful research into his methods, she attempted to restore her creator to life using a modified version of the alchemical ritual he'd used to create her. The ritual failed to bring the inventor back to life, but it did waken his corpse and instill it with an insatiable need for violence. Andora fled the estate and began a new life in Castalore.

entries.name: Catch a Killer
entries: Now Andora serves food and drink at a tavern and listens for news of the ruthless serial killer stalking the Raevan countryside with anxious guilt. She believes the murderer is none other than her accidental creation. Although Andora is reluctant to ask for help, if she discovers the player characters are also looking to find and stop the killer, she would share what she knows and join them on their mission. If the player characters help her in destroying her creation, they earn her trust— and she requests to join them for further adventures.
Now it's just a fanciful story, of course. Little girls don't turn into hulking metal monsters!

entries.name: Real Girl
entries: Llanna Deyern had an idyllic childhood. Her parents realized at a young age that she was a wechselkind, a sentient clockwork facsimile of a child, but they raised her as their own anyway. A curious and mischievous girl, she would sneak away to meet up with other children from the nearby village.

entries.name: The Twinning
entries: On one such night, Llanna met her friends in a grove rumored to be an entrance to the fey realms. As they played, thundering footsteps echoed through the forest. By the time the children ran, the towering figure broke through the trees, its antlers stretching into the sky above. Llanna doesn't remember how she escaped, but she was the only one who did.

entries.name: Living to Tell the Tale
entries: Following the loss of so many of the village youth, Llanna was exiled from her home. Ever since that event, Llanna experiences blackouts when under duress. When this happens, Llanna transforms into the Clockwork Horror, a monstrous construct bearing a striking resemblance to the {@book Great Beast|GrimHollowMG24|1|The Great Beast} that stalks the Bürach Empire. Although Llanna inconsistently recalls snippets of the Clockwork Horror's actions, she has no control over it.

entries.name: Hunting Season
entries: Llanna is terrified of her ability to transform but has resolved herself to use the Clockwork Horror to get justice for the death of her friends. She travels through the Bürach Empire in search of heroes to aid her in her quest. If the player characters agree to help her track and slay the Beast and its minions, she happily joins them, even if they have more pressing tasks to resolve first.
Her? Oh, she's a regular here. Drinks until she gets into a fight, wins more often than not, then calls it a night. Sweet girl.

entries.name: Storm Break
entries: For over a thousand years the Hammerstorm clan mined the Rock-Teeth Mountains to the east of Liesech. After the Weeping Pox swept through the city, it found its way to the Hammerstorm's underground domain, where it decimated the clan. Less than five years later, only a handful of dwarves remained. The survivors realized they could not sustain their ancestral estates and dispersed to other lands. Gillian, the youngest surviving Hammerstorm, settled in Liesech.

entries.name: Demon in a Bottle
entries: Since the dissolution of her clan, Gillian has taken to spending her inheritance drinking away her nights. She came to Liesech hopeful that she could earn a lasting heroic legacy for the Hammerstorm name, but the rampant cynicism and brutality of the city quickly wore her down—and she abandoned her dream. Quick to anger, Gillian has been kicked out of most reputable taverns in the city for starting fights, leaving her to exclusively patronize ale houses of lesser quality.

entries.name: Last Call
entries: Player characters who accomplish a heroic and public act might catch Gillian's attention, causing her to temporarily set aside her bitterness for the chance at redemption. In this circumstance, Gillian tracks the player characters down and asks to join their party. Otherwise, Gillian can be found at any of Liesech's seedy taverns, and she eagerly joins any group of adventurers that pays her tab or helps her win a bar fight.
We're out of stock on that particular item. If you don't mind who you do business with, there's a pigheaded merchant in the market right now who claims to have anything a body could want.

entries.name: Cursed Merchant
entries: Once a successful merchant in Morencia, Claunt was cursed to a porcine form by a powerful sorcerer for steeply raising the prices of staple foods during a famine. In the years since, one business venture after another failed as partners ended contracts with him out of an abundance of superstitious caution. Though not destitute, he lived far beneath the means he once enjoyed and became desperate to reverse his fortune.

entries.name: Friends in Low Places
entries: To that end, Claunt took to worshipping Gorodyn. After a particularly bad falling out with a previous business partner, Claunt made a terrible sacrifice to the Arch Daemon of Avarice and was rewarded with a {@i satchel of sorcerous sale}. With supply no longer an issue, and hoping to escape the consequences of the sacrifice, Claunt began traveling to sell his wares. It wasn't long before he discovered that even individual buyers wanted little to do with someone in his condition.

entries.name: Open for Business
entries: Now more distraught than ever, Claunt is most likely to be encountered on the road or the outskirts of major settlements, living on whatever meager sales he can make. He happily travels with any player characters agreeing to purchase his goods on an ongoing basis. If Claunt believes he can mentor (or exploit) a particularly charismatic player character, he offers to provide them with supplies if they serve as the face of his business. If he believes the player characters as a group are capable, he asks their help in tracking down and slaying the sorcerer who cursed him.
She came through two nights ago looking like she was on the run. If I'd known she was a deserter I'd have alerted the authorities straightaway, Empyreus' truth!

entries.name: Trial by Fire
entries: Having lost her parents at a young age to a wicked spellcaster, Abree Simoni dedicated her life to becoming an inquisitor. Her diligent study of Empyreus' dogma earned her the privilege of joining the Inquisition's ranks at fourteen years old. Over the course of her first years in the Arcanist Inquisition, Abree's faith was unwavering. When her superiors directed her to root out corruption, she did so with meticulous fervor When asked to put fire to the kindling beneath accused spellcasters, she did so without hesitation. Then she was deployed to the village where she and her brother had been raised by distant family following the death of their parents. She discovered her brother was accused of casting magic. Her unit was charged with incarcerating him until his trial and execution.

entries.name: Crisis of Faith
entries: Certain her brother would never practice magic, Abree tried to find proof of his innocence. When she couldn't, turning up witnesses who'd seen him engage in ritual bloodletting, she did the unthinkable—she betrayed the Inquisition and fled town with her brother. They were on the run only a few days when Abree woke in the middle of the night to find her brother had left her on her own. With no answers to the accusation against her brother, and no excuse for her superiors, Abree continued her flight from the region.

entries.name: Lost and Alone
entries: Now Abree travels from town to town, trying to make her way out of the Castinellan Provinces unnoticed. Should the player characters win Abree's trust by showing her kindness, she requests their assistance. Her first goal is to find her brother and ensure his safety. She also wishes to flee the lands where the Arcanist Inquisition holds power. If anyone among the player characters is an inquisitor, she changes her plans and asks if they could vouch for her so she can return to her post. Regardless, if the player characters agree to assist Abree in keeping her safe from the Inquisition, she happily travels with and fights alongside them.
They say the Thorn of Sante Viegre has killed a hundred inquisitors. It's probably closer to ten, but I can't deny that the brutality of his attacks seems to resonate with the common folk.

entries.name: Family Secrets
entries: Grimaldo Simoni and his older sister were raised by cousins. Told from an early age that his parents had been killed by a warlock, Grimaldo was tortured by the sense of loss. A sensitive child, he noticed that older villagers seemed to make a point of avoiding him and his sister. Only after his sister left to join the Arcanist Inquisition did he discover the reason late one night when his cousins had been drinking: his parents were not killed by a spellcaster but had been accused of being spellcasters themselves and subsequently put to death by inquisitors.

entries.name: Thicker Than Blood
entries: Shortly after learning the truth of his parents' deaths, Grimaldo began to study the occult. He became obsessed with following in his parents' footsteps and avenging them by bringing pain and suffering to as many inquisitors as he could. When a traveling merchant came through his village with a book on blood magic, Grimaldo spent everything he had to buy it. Eventually rumors began to spread around his village that he was pursuing the arcane arts. The Inquisition was notified. He was captured easily and assumed he would die. His sister, however, who had joined the Inquisition, was charged with detaining him while he awaited his trial. She released him and led him away from sure death. For a few days they traveled together, but Grimaldo knew that she could not travel the same path as he. He snuck away from her and forged his own trail.

entries.name: The Thorn of Sante Viegre
entries: Grimaldo spends his time continuing to hone his mastery of blood magic and carrying out guerilla attacks on small units of inquisitors in the Sante Viegre region of the Castinellan Provinces. He shows no mercy in these attacks, killing inquisitors and those who help them hunt arcanists. He leaves his victims in a state that shocks and terrifies anyone finding them. Grimaldo has many associates among wine smugglers in the area and is always looking to recruit those who might be sympathetic to his cause. If the player characters are known to dislike the Inquisition, or have a run in with inquisitors, Grimaldo might approach them to join forces.
Dragons are rare, so seeing one is terrifying. Seeing one rotting before your eyes as it approaches? Mind-shattering!

entries.name: Cursed Dragon
entries: The {@spell Curse of Foul Blight|GrimHollowPG24} is potent necromancy when leveled against a hated foe. Some foolish wizard modified the {@spell Curse of Foul Blight|GrimHollowPG24} to affect a dragon who had wronged them, using a single scale from the hated dragon. What this wizard unleashed was a variant of the {@spell Curse of Foul Blight|GrimHollowPG24} known as blightscale, accursed magic that twists and warps a majestic dragon into a loathsome, terrifying beast.

Humanoids infected with the {@spell Curse of Foul Blight|GrimHollowPG24} become lone wanderers, but the lairing instinct of the blightscale dragon remains true. The dragon no longer bothers to amass wealth or hunt. It makes forays from its living tomb to assuage its suffering by lashing out against any who travel nearby.

entries.name: Physical Changes
entries: A blightscale dragon's breath weapon is a stinking exhalation of insects and necrotic bile. The dragon's armorlike scales split and encrust with thick pus that hardens to the consistency of stone. Fangs crack and rot at the gums, just as deadly but crawling with disease and stained with ichor.

entries.name: Salvage
entries: Someone who has proficiency with {@item Smith's Tools|XPHB} or {@item Leatherworker's Tools|XPHB} can fashion the scales of an adult or ancient blightscale dragon into {@item Blightscale Dragon Scale Mail|GrimHollowMG24|Dragon Scale Mail}. Doing so takes 10 days and other materials worth 5,000 GP. The armor offers no protection against Frightful Presence but grants {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Necrotic damage and Poison damage.

A claw from a young or older blightscale dragon can be fashioned into a {@item Dagger of Venom|XDMG}. Doing so requires proficiency with {@item Smith's Tools|XPHB} or {@item Woodcarver's Tools|XPHB}, 5 days of work, and other materials worth 1,000 GP.

If the two eye-fangs of an adult or ancient blightscale dragon are treated with a {@spell Remove Curse|XPHB} spell and buried together in soil consecrated with a {@spell Hallow|XPHB} spell, they grow into a {@creature Blight Drake|GrimHollowMG24} that emerges from the earth in 3 days. This drake bonds with the first creature to feed it.

Blood and other fluids of a blightscale dragon can be harvested and used to offset the shadowsteel component when bestowing a {@spell Curse of Foul Blight|GrimHollowPG24}. A wyrmling is worth 250 GP, a young dragon 750 GP, an adult 1,500 GP, and an ancient dragon 3,000 GP.

entries.name: {@dc 15} {@homebrew Intelligence|Wisdom} ({@skill History}):
entries: A blightscale dragon is a dragon that has been subjected to a powerful curse.

entries.name: {@dc 15} Wisdom ({@skill Medicine}):
entries: A blightscale dragon is {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB|Immune} to Necrotic damage and Poison. Its breath weapon is a swarm of poisonous insects and gas.

entries.name: {@dc 20} Intelligence ({@skill Arcana}):
