Zabriel's own dereliction of duty encouraged her servitor angels to follow suit, and they pursued their own truth-seeking agendas as they served court mages, judges, and would-be philosopher-kings.

entries.name: A Separate Agenda
entries: The longer they spend away from the divine presence the gods, the more uncompromising and fervent Zabrielite truthseekers become. The act of uncovering secret cabals and conspiracies has taken on a life of its own; they search for truth for its own sake and not in service to the higher good. They're just as likely to attempt to reveal the secrets of incautious adventurers as they are the machinations of evil-doers.

entries.name: Salvage
entries: Four Zabrielite truthseekers' plumage is a key ingredient in {@item Wings of Flying|XDMG}. A proficient weaver can spend 7 days and 2500 GP of components to craft those wings with a successful {@dc 20} Intelligence ({@skill Arcana}) check.

entries.name: {@dc 10} Intelligence ({@skill History}):
entries: The avian creature known as the truthseeker ferrets out large conspiracies, using their abilities to slay the transgressors. They are often overzealous in their pursuits.

entries.name: {@dc 15} Intelligence ({@skill Religion}):
entries: Truthseekers were servants of the gods, but they are tied directly to Zabriel, the Arch Seraph of Truth.

entries.name: {@dc 20} Intelligence ({@skill Arcana}):
entries: The eyes of the truthseeker can shoot radiant bolts, and its gaze can transfix those who the creature attacks.

entries.name: GM Advice:
entries: A truthseeker can act as an ally to characters one moment, and an enemy the next, depending on the moral and ethical decisions and makeup of the adventuring party. Accepting the assistance of a truthseeker comes with certain risks and obligations.

legendaryGroup.name: Blightscale Dragon
lairActions: On {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} count 20 (losing {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} ties), an adult or ancient blightscale dragon takes a lair action to cause one of the following effects. The dragon can't use the same effect two rounds in a row.

items: Insects burst from a hive built on a surface that the dragon can see within 120 feet of it. Any creature within a 20-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} centered on the hive must succeed on a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw, taking 10 ({@damage 3d6}) Piercing damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. A creature that ends its turn in the cloud takes 10 ({@damage 3d6}) Piercing damage. The insects disperse on {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} count 20 of the next round. They disperse early if subjected to intense wind or a damaging area effect.

Puslike fluid erupts in a 20-foot radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} centered on a point on the ground the dragon can see within 120 feet of it. That area becomes {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB}, and a creature moving through this terrain must succeed on a {@dc 15} Strength saving throw or have the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition (escape {@dc 15}) and have the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition while grappled in this way. Another creature can pull a {@condition Restrained|XPHB} target free by taking an action and succeeding on a {@dc 15} Strength (Athletics) check. The ground dries sufficiently to return to normal on {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} count 20 of the next round.

Foul gas belches forth in a 20-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} from a point the dragon can see within 120 feet of it. The cloud spreads around corners, and its area is {@variantrule Heavily Obscured|XPHB}. Each creature within the cloud at the start of its turn must make a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the creature has the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition. Creatures that don't need to breathe or are immune to Poison damage automatically succeed on this saving throw. This cloud disperses on {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} count 20 of the next round. It disperses early if subjected to intense wind.

regionalEffects: An adult or ancient blightscale dragon's curse warps the land around the dragon's lair, creating one or more of the following effects.

items: The smell of decay can be detected 6 miles from the lair, growing stronger as one moves nearer.

Water sources within 1 mile of the lair have soured, and the water smells and tastes foul. Only the dragon can drink it safely. Any other creature that does so must succeed on a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw 10 minutes later or have the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition. A poisoned creature can repeat the saving throw every hour, ending the effect on itself on a success. A creature that succeeds on a {@dc 15} Wisdom ({@skill Medicine|XPHB}) check can cure this condition with 10 minutes of effort.
Vegetation within 1 mile of the lair is sickly and nasty. It provides no more than half its normal food value. Within 1,500 feet of the lair, plants are inedible, and no wildlife can be found.

Beasts and Plant creatures within 1 mile of the lair are aggressive and hard to reason with. In combat, at the start of its turn, such a creature gains {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on all melee attack rolls it makes during that turn, but attack rolls against it have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} until the start of its next turn. Effects that cure disease can end this effect for 1 day, but it returns if the creature remains in the area.

regionalEffects: If the dragon dies, these effects fade over {@dice 2d4} days. Signs of environmental damage remain indefinitely.

legendaryGroup.name: Gegazol
lairActions: Gegazol's lair has gained a life of its own, thanks in part to the influence of the daemon that corrupts her mind and spirit. Once per round, the daemonic presence in the lair causes an effect from the list below.

items: Tendrils of necrotic power rise and reach for living creatures within the lair. Each living creature in the lair must attempt a {@dc 21} Constitution saving throw or take 13 ({@damage 3d8}) Necrotic damage and gain one {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} level until {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} count 20 on the next round.

Gegazol crashes her wings upon the ground, and rime spreads in a sphere with a 120-foot radius around her. This sphere spreads around corners and creates {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB} made of ice. Creatures in that area must make a {@dc 21} Constitution saving throw. Those who fail take 18 ({@damage 4d8}) Cold damage and have the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition (escape {@dc 21}). On a success, the creature takes half the damage and isn't grappled. The {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB} disappears on {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} count 20 on the next round.

Gegazol creates an opaque wall of black ice on a solid surface she can see within 120 feet of her. The wall is made up of twenty contiguous 10-foot square panels that are each 2 feet thick. When the wall appears, each creature in its area is pushed 5 feet out of the wall's space, appearing on whichever side of the wall the creature wants. Each 10-foot section of the wall has {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB|AC} 5, 60 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, {@variantrule Vulnerability|XPHB} to Fire damage, and {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to Acid, Cold, Necrotic, Poison, and Psychic damage. The wall disappears when Gegazol uses this Lair Action again or when she dies.

legendaryGroup.name: Memori Lich
regionalEffects: The region containing a memori lich's lair takes on the horrible and powerful essence of its master, creating the following effects:

items: When the memori lich become {@status Bloodied|XPHB}, the ground within 120 feet of the creature shakes violently. Each creature on the memori lich's choice in that area must succeed on a {@dc 20} Strength saving throw or have the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition.
All creatures within a mile of the lair only regain half as many {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} from magical healing.

When a creature of the memori lich's choice becomes {@status Bloodied|XPHB}, ghostly hands emerge from the ground. The creature must succeed on a {@dc 20} Strength saving throw or have the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition (escape {@dc 18}). Once the creature succeeds on the saving throw or escapes, the hands disappear.

legendaryGroup.name: Puck
regionalEffects: A natural environment containing a puck's lair is warped by the puck's presence, which creates one or more of the following effects:

items: Plants within 1 mile of the lair darken in hue and look underwatered. Sap from these plants has a bloodlike tinge. This sap seeps from oddly shaped holes that resemble parts of despairing faces or wounds. Fruits are deformed and oddly colored but are otherwise normal in taste and nutrition. Traversing this area is hard and time consuming—roots trip, vines block the way, underbrush is dense and thorny.

Animals and other wild creatures within 1 mile of the lair are unnaturally aggressive, especially toward creatures hostile to the puck. However, animals are absent within 600 feet of the lair. The puck uses them to fertilize the grove

Creatures that spend 10 minutes or more within this area feel their negative emotions heightened. Effects that cause debilitating negative emotions double in duration. Effects that provide bravery, joy, hope, and similar emotions, such as the spells {@spell Aid|XPHB} or {@spell Heroism|XPHB}, halve in duration. A {@spell Calm Emotions|XPHB} spell ends either effect for that spell's normal duration.

legendaryGroup.name: Vampires
lairActions: Adult and ancient vampires form a supernatural bond with their lair, allowing them lair actions. On initiative count 20 (losing initiative ties), such vampires can take a lair action to cause one of the following effects. The vampire can't use the same effect two rounds in a row:

items: A {@creature Swarm of Insects|XMM} forms within 60 feet of the vampire. The vampire commands the swarm, which takes its turn just after the vampire. The swarm of insects remains until it is dispersed or until the vampire drops to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}.

Thorny vines or bloody tenatcles erupt from the ground in a 30-foot radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} centered on a point the vampire chooses within 120 feet of the vampire. This area is {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB} for creatures of the vampire's choice. When the impediments appear, each creature of the vampire's choice in the area must succeed on a {@dc 15} Strength saving throw or gain the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition (escape {@dc 15}) and {@condition Restrained|XPHB} while grappled in this way. The plants or tentacles retreat into the ground when the vampire drops to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}.

30-foot-radius {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation}. The mist spreads around corners, and its area is {@variantrule Heavily Obscured|XPHB} for all creatures except the vampire. It lasts until the vampire uses a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to dismiss it, the vampire drops to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, or a wind of moderate or greater speed (at least {@dice 1d4 * 10} miles per hour) disperses it.

regionalEffects: The power of an adult or ancient vampire's curse changes the region surrounding the vampire's lair, creating any of the following effects:

items: Biting insects and their larvae increase noticeably in population within 1 mile of the lair.
Unnerving natural sounds—twigs breaking, leaves crunching, trees creaking, and so on—are louder within 1 mile of the lair.
Plants within 500 feet of the lair are tougher, twisted, and any thorns or thistles they have are longer and more painful when they prick.
The air within 500 feet of the lair is noticeably colder and smells faintly of blood. This cooling of the air makes mist more likely around the lair.

regionalEffects: If the vampire dies, these effects linger for one week, then dissipate over {@dice 1d6} days.

disease.name: Tormach's Rage
entries: The bugbear has a disease called {@disease Tormach's Rage|GrimHollowMG24} that changes its alignment to Chaotic Evil, gives it the Reckless Attack trait, and grants it an extra 2 ({@damage 1d4}) damage on Strengthbased attacks (included in the attack).

A bugbear that touches the rage-infected bugbear is subjected to the following effect. {@actSave con} {@dc 14}. {@actSaveFail} The target contracts {@disease Tormach's Rage|GrimHollowMG24}. Only powerful magic like a {@spell Greater Restoration|XPHB} spell or a special herbal concoction (see Salvage) can cure this disease.
Tormach subverted bugbears with a disease that makes them little more than killing machines.

entries.name: Tormach's Rage
entries: The bugbears of Etharis weren't always ferocious and violent. When the Arch Daemons appeared in the world, Tormach believed he could make minions of the goblinoids. Bugbear warriors forcefully opposed his efforts to subvert goblin tribes to his will. Tormach commanded his servants to craft a disease that would render defiant bugbears into vicious drones, a grim task those minions completed with great success. {@disease Tormach's Rage|GrimHollowMG24}, as the disease has come to be known, spread quickly. Most bugbears of Etharis are now victims of this mind-altering plague.

The disease causes a bugbear to become more resilient while increasing muscle mass and capacity for aggression. Bugbears infected with {@disease Tormach's Rage|GrimHollowMG24} experience a powerful, instinctive urge to spread their illness. Infected bugbears have enhanced olfactory senses that allow them to smell whether a creature carries {@disease Tormach's Rage|GrimHollowMG24}. When they discover a bugbear without the disease, they fly into a frenzy. The sickness is virulent enough that even if a bugbear survives the fight or kills the infected aggressor, it's unlikely to do so without contracting {@disease Tormach's Rage|GrimHollowMG24}.

entries.name: Staged Changes
entries: Rage-infected bugbears have only recently gotten the disease. A bugbear who contracts {@disease Tormach's Rage|GrimHollowMG24} can't expect to live long as the illness compels the victim to acts of aggression that ultimately end in the bugbear's destruction. The infection lasts until death, although magic and rare herbal knowledge can counteract it.

Very rarely, an infected bugbear lives long enough that {@disease Tormach's Rage|GrimHollowMG24} exhibits advanced symptoms. At this stage, the disease further accentuates the bugbear's muscular system, granting increased strength and speed. Bugbears who live this long have little impulse control and ability to reason.

entries.name: Salvage
entries: The viscera of a bugbear infected with {@disease Tormach's Rage|GrimHollowMG24} can be used as a vector or as a curative. To create an infectious agent, someone who has proficiency with an {@item Herbalism Kit|XPHB} or a {@item Poisoner's Kit|XPHB} must use the viscera and other materials worth 25 GP. Creating the vector takes 4 hours of work and requires a successful {@dc 13} Intelligence or Wisdom check. A bugbear exposed to the vector through a wound or by consuming it must save against {@disease Tormach's Rage|GrimHollowMG24} as if bitten by a rage-infected bugbear.

Making the curative requires the same proficiency, reagents worth 50 GP, 8 hours of work, and a successful {@dc 15} Wisdom ({@skill Medicine}) check. A bugbear exposed to the curative through a wound or by consuming it can reattempt the saving throw against {@disease Tormach's Rage|GrimHollowMG24}. If the save succeeds, the disease is cured. Its effects end in a matter of minutes.

entries.name: {@dc 10} Intelligence ({@skill History} or {@skill Religion}):
entries: Bugbears once fought against the Arch Daemon Tormach's efforts to dominate goblinoids. He concocted a disease to thwart their efforts and those of any who would join them.

entries.name: {@dc 10} Intelligence ({@skill Medicine}):
entries: {@disease Tormach's Rage|GrimHollowMG24} grants its infected superior resilience and strength but causes drastic, violent personality changes.

entries.name: {@dc 15} Intelligence ({@skill Medicine}):
entries: Combined with herbal reagents, the viscera of a deceased bugbear infected with {@disease Tormach's Rage|GrimHollowMG24} can be used to spread the disease or create a cure.

entries.name: GM Advice:
entries: Bugbears fear very little, but {@disease Tormach's Rage|GrimHollowMG24} is an exception. In the few places in Etharis where bugbears have not succumbed to the disease, stopping its spread or reversing it might be one bargaining chip that characters have to strike a bargain. A more advanced version of the disease, creating an even more dangerous bugbear, is always a possibility as well.

table.name: Beast Mutations
rows: Roll twice on the table, ignoring this result if rolled again.

{@b Spawning Pit.} Your skin begins to crawl moments before vermin chew through your flesh and erupt from your body. {@dice 1d3} hostile {@creature Swarm of Crawling Claws|XMM|Swarms of Crawling Claws}, {@creature Swarm of Insects|XMM|Insects}, or {@creature Swarm of Rats|XMM|Rats} appear in unoccupied spaces within 10 feet of you. This also happens the first time you are {@status Bloodied|XPHB} after an {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} check.
{@b Bloodless.} You cannot spend {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB|Hit Dice} for any reason, including as the result of a spell or other magical effect.

{@b Corporeal Decay.} Your body begins to rapidly decay, giving you a corpselike appearance. When you gain this mutation, roll a number of {@dice d8|d8s} equal to your character level and take Necrotic damage equal to the result. You have the Undead type in addition to other creature types.

{@b Lignify.} Your limbs stiffen and woody tree shoots pierce from beneath your skin. You have {@variantrule Vulnerability|XPHB} to Fire damage and your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} (all movement types) is reduced by 5 feet per your level of Corruption.

{@b Scion of the Beast.} You carry some of the Great Beast's corruptive nature. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action on your turn, you and another creature of your choice you can see within 60 feet of you each make a Mutation check.

{@b Third Eye.} You grow a third eye. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws to avoid or remove the {@condition blinded} condition, but you have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks.

{@b Extra Digits.} You grow several extra fingers. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on checks to avoid being disarmed, but you have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Dexterity ({@skill Sleight of Hand}) checks.

{@b Chameleon.} Your skin changes color in stressful situations. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) checks, but {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Charisma checks.
{@b Oily Skin.} You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on checks made to {@action Escape a Grapple|XPHB}, but you have {@variantrule Vulnerability|XPHB} to Cold and Fire damage.

{@b Corrosive Blood.} When you become {@status Bloodied|XPHB} for the first time after an {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} roll, creatures within 5 feet of you take Acid damage equal to your character level.
{@b Sticky Feet.} You gain a {@variantrule Climb Speed|XPHB} of 15 feet, but your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is reduced by 10 feet.
{@b Luminescent.} You glow with {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} to a range of 30 feet. You have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) checks.
{@b Thinning Blood.} Your bodily fluids become thin and watery. You have the {@condition Bleeding|GrimHollowPG24} condition.

{@b Stone Shell.} Your skin swells and hardens into a tumorous rocky texture. You gain {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Piercing and Slashing damage, and {@variantrule Vulnerability|XPHB} to Acid and Thunder damage.

{@b Arcane Conduit.} Your body becomes a conduit of magic. Add your current Corruption level to your spell save {@variantrule Difficulty Class|XPHB|DC} and your spell attack rolls. When you cast a spell using a spell slot, make a Mutation check.
{@b Malformed Wings.} Spiny, tattered wings burst painfully from your back, giving you a Fly {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} of 20 feet. You cannot move by any other means.

{@b Blood-Fueled Frenzy.} If you are {@status Bloodied|XPHB}, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on melee attack rolls, and attack rolls against you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB}. On each of your turns, you must first move as far as possible toward the nearest {@status Bloodied|XPHB} creature you can see. If you are within 5 feet of it after moving, you must take the {@action Attack} action to attack that creature with a melee weapon or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}.

{@b Eldritch Combustion.} Your body fills with unstable eldritch power. If you are reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} you die instantly, and each creature within 20 feet of you must make a Mutation check.

{@b Permanent Transformation.} You gain the {@charoption Aberrant Horror|GrimHollowPG24|Aberrant Horror Transformation}. If you already have this or another Transformation, you move to the next stage of that Transformation instead.

table.name: Eldritch Effects
rows: The creature can't speak.
The creature takes 4 ({@damage 1d8}) Psychic damage at the start of each of its turns.
The creature is disoriented. It has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition and gets the condition again at the end of any turn it moves 5 feet or more.
The creature is distracted by visions and voices, and has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on attack rolls and Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks.
The creature is unsure of itself. It can move or take an action on its turn, but not both, and it can't use {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB|Reactions}.
The creature is reckless. It has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls and attack rolls against it have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB}.
The creature has the {@condition Deafened|XPHB} condition and can't see more than 30 feet away.
The creature has the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition. If the source of the condition can't be sensed, the target considers a random creature it can sense as the source of its fear.

table.name: Quake Drake Hoard
rows: Adamantine
Diamonds
Platinum
Silver
Quartz
Copper
Ruby
Electrum
Gold
Mithril
Vault Room Key

feat.name: Adroit Crafter

otherSummary.entry: Proficiency in a skill or tool

entries: You've trained in crafting items using a skill or tool that you're proficient with, and the steps in crafting have become second nature to you. You gain the following benefits:

items: The time it takes to create an item using your proficient skills or tools is halved.
If you fail to successfully craft the item and would lose the components, you retain the components instead.

feat.name: Careful Crafter

otherSummary.entry: Proficiency in a skill or tool

entries: You've trained in crafting items using a skill or tool that you're proficient with, and your creations are known for being well made and reliable. You gain the following benefits:

items: You make all ability checks for crafting with {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB}.
Anyone assisting you in crafting an item gains {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on their ability checks for crafting.
If you fail to successfully craft the item, the amount of time it took to complete the process is halved.

baseitem.name: +1 Acid Fighting Chain
entries: {@note This item has been created from Salvage text in the book; it may not be exactly as the author envisioned, some information is assumed.}

Instead, the chains can be used to make a magic chain weapon {@homebrew |(see Grim Hollow Player's Guide)}. Such a weapon offers a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls, and when the wielder hits with an attack using such a weapon, it deals {@damage 1d6} extra Acid damage. Each of these rare items requires 10 days and additional materials worth 1,000 GP to make.

item.name: +1 Chain Mail of Acid Resistance
entries: {@note This item has been created from Salvage text in the book; it may not be exactly as the author envisioned, some information is assumed.}
Made of interlocking metal rings, Chain Mail includes a layer of quilted fabric worn underneath the mail to prevent chafing and to cushion the impact of blows. The suit includes gauntlets.

Someone who has proficiency with {@item Smith's Tools|XPHB} can use the chains from one {@creature Daemon Oak|GrimHollowMG24} to fashion a {@item Chain Shirt of Acid Resistance|XDMG|Chain Shirt of Resistance (Acid)} or {@item Chain Mail of Acid Resistance|XDMG|Chain Mail of Resistance (Acid)}. These armors also offer a +1 bonus to AC.

item.name: +1 Chain Shirt of Acid Resistance
entries: {@note This item has been created from Salvage text in the book; it may not be exactly as the author envisioned, some information is assumed.}

Made of interlocking metal rings, a Chain Shirt is worn between layers of clothing or leather. This armor offers modest protection to the wearer's upper body and allows the sound of the rings rubbing against one another to be muffled by outer layers.

Someone who has proficiency with {@item Smith's Tools|XPHB} can use the chains from one {@creature daemon oak|GrimHollowMG24} to fashion a {@item Chain Shirt of Acid Resistance|XDMG|Chain Shirt of Resistance (Acid)} or {@item Chain Mail of Acid Resistance|XDMG|Chain Mail of Resistance (Acid)}. These armors also offer a +1 bonus to AC.

item.name: Adder Stone
entries: These glassy stones are often found near the place where a {@creature Marra|GrimHollowMG24} was slain. Each Adder Stone is the size of a small fruit and has a natural hole in it. While you carry an Adder Stone, the first time you would gain the {@condition Paralyzed|XPHB} condition, the stone shatters and that condition is removed.

item.name: Arcane Oil
entries: This substance is created by distilling the jelly that encases the brain of a {@creature Xakalonus|GrimHollowMG24}. You can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to coat one weapon or up to 20 pieces of ammunition. If the coated objects are not magical, for the next minute, your attacks with the coated weapon or ammunition gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls. If the coated objects are magical, they instead gain the ability to ignore the {@creature Hraptnon|GrimHollowMG24}'s {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to magical weapons.

item.name: Bat Wings of Flying
entries: {@note This item has been created from Salvage text in the book; it may not be exactly as the author envisioned, some information is assumed.}

Someone who has proficiency in {@item Leatherworker's Tools|XPHB} can make this cloak in 5 days, using reagents worth 500 GP. Somebody must cast {@spell Fly|XPHB} on the cloak on three out of the five days of its making. These wings give you a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} of only 40 feet, but they can be used once every {@dice 1d8} hours.

item.name: Blight Effigy
entries: {@note This item has been created from Salvage text in the book; it may not be exactly as the author envisioned, some information is assumed.}

Someone who has proficiency with {@item Alchemist's Supplies|XPHB}, {@skill Arcana}, {@skill Nature}, or {@skill Medicine} can remove the heart of a {@creature Blightwalker|GrimHollowMG24}. Treated with reagents worth 100 GP, wrapped in an iron chain, and dried in the sun for 3 days, the heart can become a magic item called a {@item Blight Effigy|GrimHollowMG24}. Creating this {@item Blight Effigy|GrimHollowMG24} using this process requires a successful {@dc 13} Intelligence or Wisdom check with the proficiency used. Someone holding the heart can use an action to squeeze it, casting {@spell Blight|XPHB} as a 4th-level spell with a saving throw DC of 15. Once the heart casts the spell, it and the chain binding it crumble to dust.

item.name: Blightscale Dragon Scale Mail
entries: {@note This item has been created from Salvage text in the book; it may not be exactly as the author envisioned, some information is assumed.}

Someone who has proficiency with {@item Smith's Tools|XPHB} or {@item Leatherworker's Tools|XPHB} can fashion the scales of an {@creature Adult Blightscale Dragon|GrimHollowMG24|Adult} or {@creature Ancient Blightscale Dragon|GrimHollowMG24} into {@item Dragon Scale Mail|XDMG}. Doing so takes 10 days and other materials worth 5,000 GP. The armor offers no protection against Frightful Presence but grants {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Necrotic damage and Poison damage

While wearing this armor, you gain a +1 bonus to {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB}, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against the breath weapons of Dragons, and you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to {{getFullImmRes item.resist}} damage.

Additionally, you can focus your senses as a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to discern the distance and direction to the closest {{item.detail1}} dragon within 30 miles of yourself. This action can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Bloodbonded Blood
entries: {@note This item has been created from Salvage text in the book; it may not be exactly as the author envisioned, some information is assumed.}

One vial of this blood can be harvested from a single {@creature Bloodbonded|GrimHollowMG24} with a successful {@dc 10} Wisdom ({@skill Medicine}) check. Drinking it within one day causes the user to be under the effect of a {@spell Bless|XPHB} spell. When the spell ends, the user gains 1 {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} level.

{@creature Bloodbonded|GrimHollowMG24} still possess the damaged and scarred remnants of their past devotions. When a {@creature Bloodbonded|GrimHollowMG24} dies, its blood still pulses and gives off light as it did when it was alive.

item.name: Bloodwood Spear
entries: {@note This item has been created from Salvage text in the book; it may not be exactly as the author envisioned, some information is assumed.}
The Spear inflicts the {@condition Bleeding|GrimHollowPG24} condition if targets fail a {@dc 12} Constitution saving throw {@homebrew |(see Grim Hollow Campaign Guide)}.
The massive Bloodwood Spears used by these giants are too large to be effectively wielded by Medium or smaller creatures.

However, individuals proficient in {@cite Carpenter's Tools|XPHB} who succeed on a {@dc 12} Dexterity ({@skill Nature}) check can reduce the size of the giant's spear to the equivalent of a {@item Pike|XPHB} ({@damage 1d10} Piercing damage) and which inflicts the {@condition Bleeding|GrimHollowPG24} condition if targets fail a {@dc 12} Constitution saving throw {@homebrew |(see Grim Hollow Campaign Guide)}.

item.name: Brazen Armor
entries: This armor, fashioned from the body of an {@creature Empyrean Brazen Bull|GrimHollowMG24}, gleams brightly in direct sunlight. While wearing this armor, you have a +1 bonus to {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB}. In addition, you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Fire and Radiant damage.

item.name: Cadeer Wand
entries: {@note This item has been created from Salvage text in the book; it may not be exactly as the author envisioned, some information is assumed.}

A {@creature Cadeer|GrimHollowMG24} Antler can be {@item Cadeer Wand|GrimHollowMG24|fashioned into a Wand suitable as a Spellcasting Focus} for {@item Arcane Focus|XPHB|arcane spellcasters} and {@item Druidic Focus|XPHB|druids}. If made into a magic item, this Wand has 1 extra charge per day. Also, once per day and at the cost of 1 charge thereafter, a magic Wand of Cadeer horn grants the wielder a {@dice d4} that can be rolled when the wielder makes a spellcasting ability check to see if {@spell Dispel Magic|XPHB} or {@spell Counterspell|XPHB} works. The wielder adds the number rolled on the {@dice d4} to the spellcasting ability check, or the counterspelled creature subtracts the {@dice d4} from its saving throw.
Cadeer antlers are valuable, fetching at least 75 GP each.

item.name: Cap of Bravery
entries: Woven from the remains of an {@creature Old Snarite|GrimHollowMG24}, this cap provides the wearer with a sense of magical peace and calm that emanates from them. While wearing this cap, you radiate an aura in a 30-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation}. You and allies in this aura have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws to avoid or end effects that include the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition.

item.name: Carvaloth Scale Armor
entries: This red-tinged armor made from {@creature Carvaloth|GrimHollowMG24} scales automatically shifts its color to camouflage you. When you wear this armor, Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks made to perceive you have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB}, and you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) checks.

item.name: Cauchemange Drug
entries: {@note This item has been created from Salvage text in the book; it may not be exactly as the author envisioned, some information is assumed.}

Someone who has proficiency with {@item Alchemist's Supplies|XPHB} can use powder from the corpse of one {@creature Corporeal Cauchemange|GrimHollowMG24} with other materials worth 100 GP to produce {@item Cauchemange Drug|GrimHollowMG24|a divination drug}. Doing so takes 4 hours of work and requires a successful {@dc 13} Intelligence ({@skill Arcana}) check. Inhaled while naming a subject before a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, the drug induces a nightmare-filled sleep that imparts the effect of the {@spell Legend Lore|XPHB} spell on the named subject. The user wakes with desired information but 1 {@condition Exhaustion} level.

item.name: Caustic Rapier
entries: This strange weapon is fashioned from the mandible of a {@creature Hivewolf|GrimHollowMG24}. You gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this weapon. When you roll a 19 or 20 on your attack roll with this weapon, that attack does an additional 7 ({@damage 2d6}) Acid damage. In addition, you have {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to Acid damage while wielding the weapon.

item.name: Cloak of the Listener
entries: This is a cloak made of ears that were once part of a {@creature Swarm of Ears|GrimHollowMG24}. It pulses gently and oozes yellow fluid from its many holes. While wearing this cloak, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to create one of two effects:

entries.name: Ooze
entries: You tug the cloak to create a puddle of waxy goo that covers the ground in a 10-foot square centered on a point within 30 feet of you. A creature that enters or starts its turn in the area must succeed on a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw or have the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition (escape {@dc 15}). This wax remains for 1 minute.

entries.name: Hide
entries: You completely conceal your head with the cloak. You have the {@condition Blinded|XPHB} condition, you have {@sense Blindsight|XPHB} with a range of 120 feet, and you can hear all speech within 120 feet regardless of obstructions. These effects last for 1 minute.
Once each effect is used, that effect can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Cloak of the Mausgeist
entries: This fine garment is made of {@creature Mausgeist|GrimHollowMG24} fur with a silk lining. While wearing it, you gain the following benefits:

items.name: Heightened Senses
entries: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks that rely on hearing.

items.name: Magic Protection
entries: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

items.name: Charm
entries: You can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to cast the {@spell Charm Person|XPHB} spell (save {@dc 13}). Once used, this property of the cloak can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Cloak of the Stygian Bat
entries: This cloak is made from {@creature Stygian Bat|GrimHollowMG24} hides and bristles with coarse black hair, While wearing the cloak, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks that rely on hearing.

In addition, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to gain a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} of 30 feet for one minute. While this effect is active, you also have {@sense Blindsight|XPHB} with a range of 120 feet, and you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Dexterity saving throws while in the air. Once used, this property can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Cloak of Therianthropy
entries: This spotted grey cloak is made of {@creature Styrkvisnar|GrimHollowMG24} hide. While wearing it, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Strength ({@skill Athletics}) checks.

Additionally, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to transform into a wolf, eagle, or seal for up to 1 hour. The transformation otherwise functions as the {@spell Polymorph|XPHB} spell, but you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to revert to your normal form. Once used, this property can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Coat of Lies
entries: This coat is made from the tongues of a harvester of lies' victims. While you wear it, you gain a +1 bonus to {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB} and you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Charisma ({@skill Deception}) checks.

The coat has 3 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn. You can use a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} and expend 1 charge while wearing the coat to enhance the next spell you cast before the start of your next turn, allowing that spell to be cast without any Verbal or Somatic components.

item.name: Coldrazor
entries: This infamous axe is wielded by the memori lich known as {@creature Runa Banasár|GrimHollowMG24}. It appears to be made of ice, carved with runes and studded with various tiny, glittering gems.

You gain a +3 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. On a hit, the weapon deals an extra 14 ({@damage 4d6}) Cold damage. If a single attack with Coldrazor deals more than 20 points of Cold damage, the target must succeed on a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw or have the {@condition Stunned|XPHB} condition until the end of its next turn.
Immediately after hitting or missing with a ranged attack made with this weapon, it flies back to your hand.

item.name: Corrupted Tusk-Dagger
entries: This Dagger is fashioned from a {@creature Dragonborn Vampire (Young)|GrimHollowMG24|Dragonborn Vampire}'s fangs. When a wielder scores a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} with the Dagger, the weapon damage is {@damage 4d4} instead of {@damage 2d4}. In addition, on a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB}, the target must succeed on a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw or have the {@condition Stunned|XPHB} condition until the end of its next turn.

item.name: Crown of the Keppmir King
entries: This platinum crown is adorned with black gems and shells. While you wear the crown, you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Cold damage and can breathe underwater. Also, your Constitution is 20 while you wear the crown, unless it is 20 or higher without it.

entries.name: Curse
entries: This crown is cursed, a fact revealed only when an {@spell Identify|XPHB} spell is cast on the crown or you attune to it. {@variantrule Attunement|XPHB|Attuning} to the crown curses you until you are targeted by the {@spell Remove Curse|XPHB} spell or similar magic; it is impossible to remove the crown. While cursed, you turn into a keppmir after {@dice 2d10} days.

item.name: Ebon Armor of Invulnerability
entries: {@note This item has been created from Salvage text in the book; it may not be exactly as the author envisioned, some information is assumed.}

The Plate Armor of an {@creature Ebon Knight|GrimHollowMG24} roils with the energy of undying evil. Someone who has proficiency with {@item Smith's Tools|XPHB} can melt the armor of three {@creature Ebon Knight|GrimHollowMG24|Ebon Knights} to make {@item Ebon Armor of Invulnerability|GrimHollowMG24|Armor of Invulnerability} that also grants {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Necrotic damage. However, this wicked armor requires an attuned wearer to sacrifice life force to it. After the wearer finishes a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, or the first time they put the armor on after having not worn it while finishing one or more Long Rests, they must either give up one-quarter of their remaining Hit Dice to the armor (rounded up) or increase their {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} level by 1. Someone proficient with {@item Smith's Tools|XPHB} takes 30 days to make this armor, which requires other materials worth 10,000 GP to craft. The intended first wearer must give blood each day, losing Hit Dice or taking {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} levels as if wearing the armor. The process fails, consuming half the materials, if the wearer doesn't feed the armor as it's made. If this being then fails to wear and feed the armor daily for 30 days after it's complete, it disintegrates into ash and smoke.

item.name: Elixir of Elemental Spittle, Fireborn
entries: This foul-smelling liquid is made from the saliva of a {@creature Fireborn Troll|GrimHollowMG24}. For one minute after consuming the potion, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to spit energy at a creature within 5 feet of you. The target must succeed on a {@dc 12} Dexterity saving throw or take {@damage 2d6} Fire damage. No damage is inflicted on a successful save.
If the target rolls a natural 20 on its saving throw, the potion explodes inside you. You take {@damage 6d6} damage of the type listed for it, and the potion's duration ends immediately.

item.name: Elixir of Elemental Spittle, Seaborn
entries: This foul-smelling liquid is made from the saliva of a{@creature Seaborn Troll|GrimHollowMG24} For one minute after consuming the potion, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to spit energy at a creature within 5 feet of you. The target must succeed on a {@dc 12} Dexterity saving throw or take 2d6 Acid damage. No damage is inflicted on a successful save.
If the target rolls a natural 20 on its saving throw, the potion explodes inside you. You take {@damage 6d6} damage of the type listed for it, and the potion's duration ends immediately.

item.name: Elixir of Elemental Spittle, Stoneborn
entries: This foul-smelling liquid is made from the saliva of a {@creature Stoneborn Troll|GrimHollowMG24}. For one minute after consuming the potion, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to spit energy at a creature within 5 feet of you. The target must succeed on a {@dc 12} Dexterity saving throw or take 2d6 Bludgeoning damage. No damage is inflicted on a successful save.
If the target rolls a natural 20 on its saving throw, the potion explodes inside you. You take {@damage 6d6} damage of the type listed for it, and the potion's duration ends immediately.

item.name: Elixir of Elemental Spittle, Windborn
entries: This foul-smelling liquid is made from the saliva of a {@creature Windborn Troll|GrimHollowMG24}. For one minute after consuming the potion, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to spit energy at a creature within 5 feet of you. The target must succeed on a {@dc 12} Dexterity saving throw or take 2d6 Lightning damage . No damage is inflicted on a successful save.
If the target rolls a natural 20 on its saving throw, the potion explodes inside you. You take {@damage 6d6} damage of the type listed for it, and the potion's duration ends immediately.

item.name: Elixir of Life Protection
entries: After you drink this thick, chalky mixture, your {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximum cannot be reduced for 10 minutes.

item.name: Enchanted Feather
entries: This magical feather can sometimes be found growing from a {@creature Rabenhex|GrimHollowMG24}. While holding the feather, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to cast {@spell Remove Curse|XPHB}. Afterward, the feather becomes nonmagical.

item.name: Epachrach Scale Mail
entries: {@note This item has been created from Salvage text in the book; it may not be exactly as the author envisioned, some information is assumed.}

The leaves and bark that form an {@creature Epachrach|GrimHollowMG24}'s skin are as durable as steel but much lighter and more flexible, making them ideal for creating {@item Scale Mail|XPHB}. Someone who has proficiency with {@item Smith's Tools|XPHB} or {@item Woodcarver's Tools|XPHB} can use the hide to craft {@item Scale Mail|XPHB} that allows a wearer a maximum Dexterity bonus to AC of +3 and doesn't cause the wearer to have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) checks. This armor takes 10 days to make, requires other materials worth 250 GP, and weighs 25 pounds.

item.name: Flammable Tincture
entries: This swirling mixture of red and white liquid is formed from the remains of a {@creature Coldfire Ooze|GrimHollowMG24}. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, the vial of Flammable Tincture can be hurled up to 30 feet at a target, which must succeed on a {@dc 10} Dexterity saving throw or be covered with the liquid. For 1 minute, the creature loses its {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} or {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Fire damage and has {@variantrule Vulnerability|XPHB} to Fire damage instead.

item.name: Glass-Studded Armor
entries: While wearing this armor, you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Radiant damage. Whenever a creature hits or misses you with a melee attack, it takes 3 Piercing damage. An attacker can choose to have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on its attack roll to avoid this damage.

item.name: Gold Boar Ring
entries: The ring, made from the bristles of the {@creature Goldmane Boar|GrimHollowMG24}, sheds {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} in a 20-foot radius and {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} for an additional 20 feet. This light shines even in areas where magic does not function, or where magical light would normally be suppressed.

item.name: Gozomite Ichor (flask)
entries: {@note This item has been created from Salvage text in the book; it may not be exactly as the author envisioned, some information is assumed.}
It takes 4 hours to harvest and bottle {@dice 1d4} doses of {@item Gozomite Ichor (flask)|GrimHollowMG24|ichor from a gozomite} that did not die from Fire damage.

As an {@variantrule Action|XPHB}, you can splash the {@item Gozomite Ichor (flask)|GrimHollowMG24|gozomite ichor} onto a creature within 5 feet of you or throw a flask of it up to 20 feet, shattering it on impact. Make a ranged attack against a target creature or object, treating the ichor as an improvised weapon. On a hit, the target is covered in ichor and if the target takes any Fire damage before the ichor dries (within 3 rounds), the target is {@variantrule Vulnerability|XPHB|Vulnerable} to Fire damage.

item.name: Grenade of Remorse
entries: This substance, harvested from the tears of a {@creature Remorseful Storm|GrimHollowMG24}, proves toxic to creatures of unnatural origins. You can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to hurl the grenade at a point up to 30 feet away. When it lands, the grenade explodes in a 20-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere}. Each Aberration, Fiend, and Undead creature within the area makes a {@dc 17} Constitution saving throw, taking 8d6 Psychic damage on a failed save or half that much on a successful one.

item.name: Gristaline
entries: This gruesome mixture is made from ground {@creature Mouruge|GrimHollowMG24} bones, along with rotten meat and mud. When consumed, your Strength score becomes 19 for 1 hour (unless it is already 19 or higher). You also have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Intelligence ability checks and saving throws for the duration.

item.name: Hogs' Wash
entries: This foul-tasting tea is brewed from the powdered tusks of {@creature Doom Boar|GrimHollowMG24|Doom Boars}. When you drink this potion, you gain the effect of a {@spell Remove Curse|XPHB} spell.
Hogs' Wash can also be used as a component in removing a Shadowsteel curse (see {@book Shadowsteel Curses|GrimHollowPG24|7|Shadowsteel Curses}).

item.name: Imp Potion of Fire Resistance
entries: {@note This item has been created from Salvage text in the book; it may not be exactly as the author envisioned, some information is assumed.}

An {@creature Malikirian Imp|GrimHollowMG24|Imp}'s scales and bones can be used to create a {@item Imp Potion of Fire Resistance|GrimHollowMG24|Potion of Fire Resistance} with a {@dc 15} Intelligence ({@skill Arcana}) ability check from a character proficient in {@item Alchemist's Supplies|XPHB}. This takes 2 hours and 10 GP of materials. However, there's a {@chance 90|10% chance|Imp|The potion acts normally|The potion imparts fulnerability to all damage except fire.} that the person taking the potion will have {@variantrule Vulnerability|XPHB} to all other damage.
Eating the Malikirian imp's heart grants the ability to cast {@spell Suggestion|XPHB} once without using a spell slot. The {@variantrule Difficulty Class|XPHB|DC} to resist this spell is 10.

item.name: Ithjar Hide Armor
entries: {@note This item has been created from Salvage text in the book; it may not be exactly as the author envisioned, some information is assumed.}

Popular superstition says wearing {@creature Ithjar|GrimHollowMG24} teeth or hide imparts some of the creature's nimbleness to the wearer. This belief isn't wholly true, but someone who has proficiency with {@item Leatherworker's Tools|XPHB} can make {@item Hide Armor|XPHB} from the hide {@item Ithjar Hide Armor|GrimHollowMG24|that has a base AC of 13 and allows a wearer to apply their full Dexterity bonus to AC}. Creating this armor takes 10 days. Such {@item Stormborn Ithjar Hide Armor|GrimHollowMG24|Hide Armor} fashioned from a stormborn ithjar is {@item Armor of Resistance|XDMG|Armor of Resistance (Lightning and Thunder)}. This armor takes no longer to make, but it requires other materials worth 2,000 GP.

item.name: Javelin of Ithjar Lightning
entries: {@note This item has been created from Salvage text in the book; it may not be exactly as the author envisioned, some information is assumed.}

The central fang of a {@creature Stormborn Ithjar|GrimHollowMG24} can be fashioned into a {@item Javelin of Lightning|XDMG} that deals {@damage 2d8} Lightning damage and {@damage 2d8} Thunder damage instead of only {@damage 4d6} Lightning damage. A creature hit by the Javelin must also succeed on a {@dc 13} Strength saving throw or gain the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition. Someone who has proficiency with {@item Smith's Tools|XPHB} can make this weapon with 10 days of work and other materials worth 250 GP. Somebody must also cast {@spell Thunderwave|XPHB} on the weapon three times during its creation.

item.name: Knifetooth Studded Leather Armor
entries: {@note This item has been created from Salvage text in the book; it may not be exactly as the author envisioned, some information is assumed.}

The tattered, scorched leathers of a {@creature Knifetooth Firebrand|GrimHollowMG24} can become infused with magic. Cleaning and fitting them takes someone who has proficiency with {@item Leatherworker's Tools|XPHB} 5 days and materials worth 250 GP. The finished armor is {@item Studded Leather Armor of Fire Resistance|XDMG|Studded Leather Armor of Resistance (Fire)} that also confers a +1 bonus to AC.

item.name: Knifewing Cape
entries: This cloak is made from the skin of twenty {@creature Knifewing|GrimHollowMG24|Knifewings}. While wearing it, you gain a +1 bonus to {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB}. If you fall, you descend 60 feet per round and take no damage from falling.

item.name: Lindwyrm Venom
entries: This terrible venom is extracted and distilled from the deadly {@creature Lindwyrm|GrimHollowMG24}. It can be delivered via ingestion or a wound. A creature exposed to the venom must succeed on a {@dc 18} Constitution saving throw or take 36 ({@damage 8d8}) Poison damage and have the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition for 1 minute. While {@condition Poisoned|XPHB}, the creature has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Strength and Dexterity skill checks and saving throws.

item.name: Living Net
entries: This net is made of wire and thread from a {@creature Suture Golem|GrimHollowMG24}. It can be used in the same manner as a normal {@item Net|XPHB}, but once it restrains a target it coils and twists as if alive. Escaping the Living Net requires a {@dc 15} Strength ({@skill Athletics}) check. The Living Net has {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB|AC} 15 and 15 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|HP}. If destroyed, the net repairs itself after 1 minute.

item.name: Lycanthropy Antidote
entries: Made with {@creature Fzeg|GrimHollowMG24} blood, this silvery potion is dotted with swirling flecks of red. Drinking this liquid removes the curse of lycanthropy.

item.name: Magebane Bomb
entries: This bubbling liquid is distilled from the remains of a {@creature Magebane Ooze|GrimHollowMG24}. You can throw the container at an enemy's weapon or armor, or at a spellcaster.

Make a ranged weapon attack. On a hit, the target takes 3 ({@damage 1d6}) Acid damage. If the weapon was targeted, that weapon takes a -1 penalty to attacks and damage as well. If armor was targeted, that armor takes a -1 penalty to the {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB}. Both effects end after the wielder or wearer completes a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
A spellcaster hit with a Magebane Bomb has a -1 penalty to hit with Spell attacks, and the DCs of saving throws against spells they cast decrease by 1 for 1 minute.

item.name: Malleus Macabre
entries: This weapon is a blunt but jagged-edged bludgeon that is the object form of a {@creature Kokela|GrimHollowMG24}. You gain a +4 bonus to damage rolls made with this weapon, and attack rolls made with this weapon score a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} on a roll of 19 or 20. If you have abilities that increase your critical range with melee attack rolls, this weapon scores a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} on a roll of 17-20.

entries.name: Curse
entries: This weapon is cursed. {@variantrule Attunement|XPHB|Attuning} to it curses you until you spend one year at least a mile away from the weapon. While you remain cursed, you're unwilling to part with the club, always keeping it within reach. You also have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on attack rolls with weapons other than this one, unless no foe is within 60 feet of you that you can see or hear.

Whenever you roll a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} with this weapon, after dealing damage there is a {@chance 25|25 chance||Casts Cloudkill at level 9 centered on you|Don't casts Cloudkill at level 9 centered on you} the Malleus Macabre casts {@spell Cloudkill|XPHB} at level 9 centered on you.

item.name: Mask of the Maestro
entries: Carved from yew, this mask is shaped in the likeness of a beast's visage and has 3 charges. While wearing the mask, you can expend 1 charge to cast {@spell Polymorph|XPHB} on yourself, with a maximum duration of 1 minute.

entries.name: Curse
entries: This item is cursed. Wearing it curses you until you are targeted by a {@spell Remove Curse|XPHB} spell or similar magic. While you remain cursed, you cannot remove the mask.

item.name: Master of Ceremonies' Whip
entries: {@note This item has been created from Salvage text in the book; it may not be exactly as the author envisioned, some information is assumed.}

The {@creature Wretched Master of Ceremonies|GrimHollowMG24}' whip retains some of its magic. In the hands of a creature attuned to it, this magic whip deals an extra {@damage 2d4} Psychic damage to any target it hits.

item.name: Morose Monocle
entries: This opaque, grey eyepiece completely blocks normal vision for the eye wearing it. The only things you can see through it are grief eaters, and creatures experiencing significant pain, sorrow, or guilt. These creatures appear as white outlines, shining with an intensity that matches their emotional turmoil. The range of this unusual vision is 60 feet.
While wearing the monocle, you also have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Wisdom ({@skill Insight}) checks made to gauge a creature's emotions.

item.name: Mukad Venom
entries: A creature subjected to this poison takes {@damage 1d6} Poison damage. The creature must succeed on a {@dc 12} Constitution saving throw or gain the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition for 1 minute. The target can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. If the creature drops to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} while poisoned in this way, it becomes stable, but it is {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} for 1 hour and has the {@condition Paralyzed|XPHB} condition.

item.name: Muscle Graft
entries: Created through a gruesome process requiring the muscle tissue of a {@creature Bugbear Vampire|GrimHollowMG24}, this graft melds to the flesh of a living creature who attunes to it. Your Strength increases by 2, to a maximum of 30. When you become attuned, you must succeed on a {@dc 15} Constitution save or your Constitution decreases by 2, to a minimum of 1.

entries.name: Curse
entries: This graft is cursed. {@variantrule Attunement|XPHB|Attuning} to it curses you for 1 year or until you are targeted by {@spell Remove Curse|XPHB} or a similar spell. Removing the graft fails to end the curse on you, and you cannot end the attunement while the curse lasts. You develop a deep desire for rare meat and raw blood. At the end of each week, you must succeed on a {@dc 10} Constitution saving throw or become a {@creature Vampire Spawn|GrimHollowMG24} with no progenitor.

item.name: Necrotic Poison
entries: {@note This item has been created from Salvage text in the book; it may not be exactly as the author envisioned, some information is assumed.}

Glands within a {@creature Darkfeather|GrimHollowMG24}'s skull can be harvested and processed into one dose of a {@item Necrotic Poison|GrimHollowMG24}, which acts as a {@item Potion of Poison|XDMG} that inflicts both Poison and Necrotic damage if invested. Harvesting and preparing the poison requires proficiency with a {@item Poisoner's Kit|XPHB}, takes 4 hours, and requires a successful {@dc 12} Intelligence check.

This concoction looks, smells, and tastes like a {@item Potion of Healing|XDMG} or another beneficial potion. However, it is actually poison masked by illusion magic. {@spell Identify|XPHB} reveals its true nature.
If you drink this potion, you take {@damage 4d6} Poison damage and must succeed on a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw or have the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition for 1 hour.

item.name: Net of Maiden's Hair
entries: Sewn from the plant creature known as {@creature Maiden's Hair, Greater|GrimHollowMG24|Maiden's Hair}, this sweet-smelling piece of headgear bestows lingering magical properties. While wearing the net on your head, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Charisma ({@skill Persuasion}) checks. Additionally, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to make an ability check to end the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition, and you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on ability checks to end the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition.

item.name: Nightmare Staff
entries: This twisted staff is created from the remains of a {@creature Marra|GrimHollowMG24}. It has 3 charges and regains its expended charges daily at dusk. When a creature you can see within 60 feet of you damages you while you hold this staff, you can take a Reaction to expend 1 of the staff's charges to force the creature to make a {@dc 13} Intelligence saving throw. On a failure, the creature sees a phantasm that causes it to have the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition until the end of its next turn. If the creature is already {@condition Frightened|XPHB}, it also takes {@damage 2d6} Psychic damage.

item.name: Paralyzing Bolt
entries: These serpent tooth-tipped crossbow bolts cause a target's muscles to become rigid. When a creature takes damage from this bolt, it has the {@condition Paralyzed|XPHB} condition until the end of your next turn unless they succeed on a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw. Once it hits a target, the ammunition is no longer magical.

item.name: Periapt of Proof against Poison and Petrified
entries: {@note This item has been created from Salvage text in the book; it may not be exactly as the author envisioned, some information is assumed.}

Someone who has proficiency with {@item Alchemist's Supplies|XPHB} can take four {@creature Lupilisk|GrimHollowMG24} venom fangs, or just the two fangs of one lupilisk elder, and treat them with reagents worth 250 GP per tooth for 3 days. A successful treatment requires the alchemist to succeed on a {@dc 15} Intelligence or Wisdom check. During the treatment time, somebody must also cast {@spell Lesser Restoration|XPHB} on the fangs. After a successful treatment, someone who has proficiency with {@item Jeweler's Tools|XPHB} can fashion the fangs, now blackened, into a necklace, creating a {@item Periapt of Proof Against Poison|XDMG}. If the wearer attunes to the periapt, the amulet also provides that wearer {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to the {@condition Petrified|XPHB} condition.

item.name: Pigskin Armor
entries: This strong, flexible armor made from the hide of {@creature Ogre Swine|GrimHollowMG24} is surprisingly supple. It is considered Light armor rather than Medium armor. When a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} is scored on you, roll {@dice 1d20}. On a 17-20, you treat it as a hit instead. However, while wearing the armor, you snore uncontrollably while sleeping, and your food intake doubles.

item.name: Potion of Glacial Resistance
entries: This brew is made from the ground horns of {@creature Keppmir|GrimHollowMG24|Keppmirs}. When consumed, this potion allows you to breathe underwater for 1 hour, during which time you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Cold damage. It tastes salty and gives off a slight warmth.

item.name: Potion of Harm
entries: This concoction is the result of a failed attempt to make a Potion of Heal from the remains of a {@creature Downcast Vampire (Adult)|GrimHollowMG24|Downcast Vampire}. It is indistinguishable from that potion without using divination magic of level 3 or higher.

When you imbibe this potion, roll {@dice 1d4}. That becomes your new {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} total. If the Potion of Harm is given to a creature with 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, they instantly suffer a {@variantrule Death Saving Throw|XPHB} failure and begin dying if they are {@variantrule Stable|XPHB}.

item.name: Potion of Heal
entries: This ebon liquid is made from the remains of a {@creature Downcast Vampire (Adult)|GrimHollowMG24|Downcast Vampire}. When you imbibe this potion, you regain all your {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}.

item.name: Potion of Hungerer Poison
entries: {@note This item has been created from Salvage text in the book; it may not be exactly as the author envisioned, some information is assumed.}

Someone who has proficiency with {@item Alchemist's Supplies|XPHB} or a {@item Poisoner's Kit|XPHB} can use venom harvested from the {@creature Hungerer Snake|GrimHollowMG24} to create one {@item Potion of Poison|XDMG} that also exposes the drinker to {@disease Tears of the Hungerer|GrimHollowPG24}. Producing the poison takes 2 hours, reagents worth 50 GP, and a successful {@dc 13} Intelligence ({@skill Arcana}) check.

This concoction looks, smells, and tastes like a {@item Potion of Healing|XDMG} or another beneficial potion. However, it is actually poison masked by illusion magic. {@spell Identify|XPHB} reveals its true nature.
If you drink this potion, you take {@damage 4d6} Poison damage and must succeed on a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw or have the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition for 1 hour.

item.name: Potion of Life Sharing
entries: This shimmering liquid changes colors constantly within its vial. Made from the acid glands of the dreaded {@creature Wolfentroika|GrimHollowMG24}, this potion allows you to share your life force with others. After drinking this potion, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to choose a creature within 30 feet of you that you can see or hear. That creature regains {@dice 4d6} {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, and you take an equal amount of Necrotic damage, which can't be reduced or prevented in any way. The effect ends after 1 minute, at which point you gain 1 {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} level.

item.name: Potion of Malice
entries: This brew is made from the spore sacs of three grippers. When consumed, this potion causes the imbiber to give off spores that anger enemies within 10 feet. Those enemies must succeed on a {@dc 10} Wisdom saving throw, or they must target the imbiber with their attacks for 1 minute. All weapon attacks are made with {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB}.

item.name: Potion of Relentlessness
entries: This viscous potion is made from Quoxxarie blood. After drinking this potion, when you are reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} you can expend a {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB|Hit Die} (no action required) and set your {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} to 1. This effect lasts for 1 minute, at the end of which you must succeed on a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw or gain 1 {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} level for each {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB|Hit Die} spent during the potion's duration.

item.name: Potion of Vampire Climbing
entries: {@note This item has been created from Salvage text in the book; it may not be exactly as the author envisioned, some information is assumed.}

Someone proficient with {@item Alchemist's Supplies|XPHB} or an {@item Herbalism Kit|XPHB} can powder the vampire's claws and mix them with reagents worth 50 GP. Brewing takes 3 hours. The result is a {@item Potion of Climbing|XDMG} that also allows the drinker to climb upside down along ceilings.

item.name: Quakescale Armor
entries: Fashioned from the scales of a {@creature Quake Drake|GrimHollowMG24}, this armor rumbles softly with the sound of thunder during storms. While wearing it, you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Thunder damage.

item.name: Ready Gunk
entries: This sticky paste is fashioned from the powdered bone armor of a {@creature Bone Trader|GrimHollowMG24}. After you treat a weapon, shield, or other item that can be wielded in one or two hands with the paste, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to summon and equip it, as long as the item is within 30 feet of you. After using this effect three times, it loses its potency.

item.name: Resuscitation Bomb
entries: This foul-smelling concoction is made from the remains of stinking sludge. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can throw the vial up to 10 feet, where it breaks and releases a noxious vapor that fills a 30-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere}. Each creature in the area with the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB}, {@condition Stunned|XPHB}, or {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} condition ends those conditions. Each dying creature in the area has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on its next {@variantrule Death Saving Throw|XPHB}. Each creature that was not dying and did not have any of those conditions must instead succeed on a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw or have the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition for 1 minute.

item.name: Ring of Nightmares
entries: This ring's crafting requires powdered brain from a {@creature Restless Lumberer|GrimHollowMG24}. It has 3 charges and regains {@dice 1d3} expended charges daily at dawn. While wearing the ring, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to expend 1 charge to change the damage type of the next spell you cast before the end of your turn to Psychic damage.
In addition, while attuned to the ring, you're immune to spells or magical abilities that interfere with your sleep or cause nightmares while you sleep.

item.name: Sizzling Serum
entries: Created from the ichor in a {@creature Potbelly|GrimHollowMG24}'s stomach-pot, this volatile concoction is effective at damaging inorganic substances. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can hurl the serum at a Construct or an object that you can see within 30 feet of you. The target makes a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw, taking 10d6 Fire damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.

item.name: Slugger (vial)
entries: {@note This item has been created from Salvage text in the book; it may not be exactly as the author envisioned, some information is assumed.}

{@creature Putrefaction Slug|GrimHollowMG24|Putrefaction Slugs} ignore what they can't devour, making any treasure they possess incidental. But the glands that generate their digestive acid are prized by alchemists.

Removing an intact slug gland requires a successful {@dc 12} Intelligence ({@skill Nature}) or Wisdom ({@skill Medicine}) check. Once removed, the gland must be stored in alcohol or similar preservative agents. Using four such glands, a person can create a more potent version of acid that renders a target that takes damage from the acid have {@variantrule Vulnerability|XPHB} to Bludgeoning damage until the end of the thrower's next turn.

These enhanced acids are called {@item Slugger (Vial)|GrimHollowMG24|Sluggers}, cost 40 GP, and weigh the same as a standard {@item Acid|XPHB|Vial of Acid}. Creating a {@item Slugger (vial)|GrimHollowMG24|Slugger} requires proficiency with {@item Alchemist's Supplies|XPHB} and a successful {@dc 13} Intelligence check to use them in a process that takes 4 hours. Failure creates normal acid, while failure by 5 or more renders a useless result.

item.name: Staff of Briars
entries: This wooden staff is made from the harvested claws of a {@creature Braerog|GrimHollowMG24}. It has 10 charges. While holding the staff, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to expend 1 charge and cause thorn-covered rose vines to sprout up, covering a 20-foot square centered on a point within 5 feet of you. This area becomes {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB}.
Once created, the briars are non-magical. They grow (or wither) as appropriate for the environment, just as a normal plant would.
The staff regains {@dice 1d6 + 4} expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the last charge, roll {@dice 1d20}. On a 1, the staff turns into rose petals and is lost forever.

item.name: Stormborn Ithjar Hide Armor
entries: {@note This item has been created from Salvage text in the book; it may not be exactly as the author envisioned, some information is assumed.}

Popular superstition says wearing {@creature Ithjar|GrimHollowMG24} teeth or hide imparts some of the creature's nimbleness to the wearer. This belief isn't wholly true, but someone who has proficiency with {@item Leatherworker's Tools|XPHB} can make {@item Hide Armor|XPHB} from the hide {@item Ithjar Hide Armor|GrimHollowMG24|that has a base AC of 13 and allows a wearer to apply their full Dexterity bonus to AC}. Creating this armor takes 10 days. Such {@item Stormborn Ithjar Hide Armor|GrimHollowMG24|Hide Armor} fashioned from a {@creature Stormborn Ithjar|GrimHollowMG24} is {@item Armor of Resistance|XDMG|Armor of Resistance (Lightning and Thunder)}. This armor takes no longer to make, but it requires other materials worth 2,000 GP.

item.name: Suit of the Lamb
entries: This suit of comfortable woolen clothes, made from the wool of the rare and dangerous poffer, includes a shirt, trousers or a kilt, and thick socks. While wearing the suit, you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Lightning damage, and you don't suffer effects from {@hazard Extreme Cold|XDMG|Extreme Cold weather}.

item.name: Talisman of Tormach
entries: The dreadful talisman is crafted from the skull at the center of a {@creature Gore Elemental|GrimHollowMG24}. When you kill a creature with a Melee weapon or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} while you wear the talisman, you gain 10 {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} for 1 minute. While you have any of these {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB}, your melee weapon attacks and {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB|Unarmed Strikes} deal an extra {@damage 1d6} Necrotic damage on a hit.

item.name: Telltale Heart
entries: This cherry-sized bloodstone heart is the beating center of a poppet. While the stone is on your person, when you gain the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition, you can make a {@dc 15} Wisdom saving throw. On a success, you end the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition.

Also, when you make an Intelligence ability check while holding the stone, you can listen to it, gaining unusual insight or access to esoteric lore. If you do so, you gain {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on that Intelligence check. The Telltale Heart can't be used this way again until the next dawn.

item.name: Vampire Ointment
entries: {@note This item has been created from Salvage text in the book; it may not be exactly as the author envisioned, some information is assumed.}

Someone proficient with {@item Alchemist's Supplies|XPHB} or {@item Cook's Utensils|XPHB} can render a {@creature Vampire Spawn|GrimHollowMG24} or older vampire's flesh in a manner like that done to produce candle tallow. Doing so for one vampire requires 1 day of work, other materials worth 50 GP, and a successful {@dc 13} Intelligence or Wisdom check. The result from a typical vampire is three applications of an ointment. Applying the ointment takes 1 minute and causes any vampire to have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks to sense the user. Also, the ointment repels insects and arachnids, who have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks and attack rolls against the user. They usually leave the user alone. The user also has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against magically produced insects, such as the {@spell Insect Plague|XPHB} spell. One application lasts 1 hour.

item.name: Wand of Silence
entries: This wand has 7 charges. While holding it, you can expend 1 charge to cast {@spell Silence|XPHB} from it (save {@dc 15}). The wand regains {@dice 1d6 + 1} expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the wand's last charge, roll a {@dice d20}. On a 1, the wand crumbles into ashes and is destroyed.

itemGroup.name: Elixir of Elemental Spittle
entries: This foul-smelling liquid is made from the saliva of an Elemental Troll. For one minute after consuming the potion, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to spit energy at a creature within 5 feet of you. The target must succeed on a {@dc 12} Dexterity saving throw or take {@damage 2d6} damage of a type corresponding to the troll whose spittle was used to craft the potion. No damage is inflicted on a successful save.
If the target rolls a natural 20 on its saving throw, the potion explodes inside you. You take {@damage 6d6} damage of the type listed for it, and the potion's duration ends immediately.
{@note This item has been created from Salvage text in the book; it may not be exactly as the author envisioned, some information is assumed.}
A creature wearing the armor has advantage on saving throws against disease.

Someone who has proficiency with {@item Leatherworker's Tools|XPHB} can fashion the hides of ten {@creature Pox Dog|GrimHollowMG24|Pox Dogs} into {@item Blight Leather Armor|GrimHollowMG24|Leather Armor}, {@item Blight Studded Leather Armor|GrimHollowMG24|Studded Leather Armor}, or {@item Blight Hide Armor|GrimHollowMG24|Hide Armor}. Leather and Hide require 8 days of work and extra materials worth 50 GP, while studded leather requires 10 days and 100 GP. Somebody must cast {@spell Lesser Restoration|XPHB} on the armor at least once during the process. A creature wearing the armor has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against disease.
{@note This item has been created from Salvage text in the book; it may not be exactly as the author envisioned, some information is assumed.}
You have a {=bonusAc} bonus to AC while wearing this armor.

An accomplished leatherworker can fashion the callused skin of a {@creature Chapped Brute|GrimHollowMG24} into +1 Armor—{@item Chapped Brute Leather Armor|GrimHollowMG24|Leather}, {@item Chapped Brute Studded Leather Armor|GrimHollowMG24|Studded Leather}, or {@item Chapped Brute Hide Armor|GrimHollowMG24|Hide}—{@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Necrotic damage and doesn't require {@variantrule Attunement|XPHB}.
This armor takes 10 extra days of work to fashion, and magical components worth 100 GP must be expended in its creation.
{@note This item has been created from Salvage text in the book; it may not be exactly as the author envisioned, some information is assumed.}

The spine spikes of a {@creature Venomous Gnoll|GrimHollowMG24} can be fashioned into {@item Gnoll Ammunition|GrimHollowMG24|four arrows, bolts, or darts}. Someone who has proficiency with {@item Smith's Tools|XPHB} or {@item Tinker's Tools|XPHB} can make these projectiles in 4 hours. Each time one of these projectiles hits a target, the target takes an extra {@damage 1d4} Poison damage, but if the {@dice d20} on the attack roll is a 1 or an even number other than 20, the projectile ceases to be poisonous.
{@note This item has been created from Salvage text in the book; it may not be exactly as the author envisioned, some information is assumed.}

A central spike growing from the {@creature Hraptnon|GrimHollowMG24} can be fashioned into a Spear or Lance by a proficient Weaponsmith succeeding on a {@dc 20} Intelligence ({@skill Arcana}) check. This takes 10 days of work and 1,000 GP of components. The {@item Hraptnon Weapon|GrimHollowMG24|Spear or Lance} acts as a {@item +3 Weapon|XDMG} that does an additional 7 ({@damage 2d6}) Force damage on a successful hit. This magical weapon can affect the {@creature Hraptnon|GrimHollowMG24}, unlike other magical weapons.

Made from the claws, teeth, and bones of lycanthropes, these weapons are particularly effective against other lycanthropes or vampires. You gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

Additionally, on a successful attack against a lycanthrope, the target is forced back into its Humanoid form unless it succeeds on a {@dc 10} Charisma saving throw. Also, damage done to vampires with Lycan Weapons cannot be regenerated until the creature finishes a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}.

Dedicated followers of Tormach sometimes gain the blessing of the Lord of Blood Oaths. When they do, their weapons gain a spark of the Marauder's power. When you hit with this weapon, you can cause the target to succeed on a {@dc 15} Strength saving throw or have the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition. Once a creature fails this save, you can't use this ability again until the next dawn.
At each dawn, roll {@dice 1d20}. On a 1 or 2 the blessing ends, removing this effect from the weapon.

entries.name: Introduction
entries: {@i Grim Hollow: The Monster Grimoire} is the third installment in the Grim Hollow series of setting books. Two other tomes—{@loader Grim Hollow: The Campaign Guide|collection/Ghostfire Gaming; Grim Hollow - Campaign Guide - 2024.json} and {@loader Grim Hollow: The Players Guide|collection/Ghostfire Gaming; Grim Hollow - Player's Guide - 2024.json}—preceded it. The three books form the complete set of books for gamemasters to run dark fantasy and horror campaigns in the Grim Hollow setting.

The Grim Hollow setting takes place in a world known as Etharis, a dark and gritty environment where the vicious, terrible, horrifying monsters that hide in the shadowy alleyways and remote wildernesses are only the start of the peril. As much as the monsters of the world threaten the citizenry, the dark figures that entice those who seek power are just as dangerous.
